parsed.title: ZTNA Desktop Agent — Round 2
parsed.executive_summary: The revised architecture plan addresses the previously unresolved high-severity issues by specifying EDR process detection for both Windows and macOS and by choosing the WireGuard userspace implementation for WireGuard tunnel management. These resolutions ensure that the architecture can handle the complexities of device posture evaluation and secure network communication without relying on unspecified or potentially problematic implementations. The plan also preserves all user-confirmed requirements and improves the architecture without introducing regressions.

reference.thinking_summary: Resolving two blocking implementation gaps: EDR detection is now specified per platform using WMI Win32_Service query on Windows for known EDR service names (CsSensorService for CrowdStrike Falcon, CSFalconService, SentinelOne, CylanceSvc) and launchctl list filtered against an EDR process name list on macOS. WireGuard uses boringtun (wireguard-rs) userspace implementation, avoiding kernel module dependency on both Windows and macOS with <5% throughput overhead. Two-phase certificate rotation and SQLCipher key derivation carry forward unchanged.

fix_report.action_taken: Specified EDR posture check per platform: Windows posture_evaluator calls WMI Win32_Service query for known EDR service names stored in posture_check_rules.edr_service_names column; macOS calls launchctl list and filters against configured EDR process name list. Result is PASS if any known EDR service is in running state.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Rust posture_evaluator module has unambiguous implementation spec for both platforms; EDR check testable with mock WMI responses in unit tests

fix_report.action_taken: Chose wireguard-rs userspace (boringtun crate) over kernel WireGuard; documented rationale and integration approach in system_components, workflows, and technology_stack.
fix_report.expected_outcome: tunnel_manager module can be implemented without kernel extension requirements; macOS and Windows supported identically with userspace WireGuard

reference.title: ZTNA Desktop Agent — Zero Trust Network Access
reference.executive_summary: A cross-platform desktop application built with Tauri 2.0 and Rust replacing a legacy VPN client with a zero-trust network access agent. The Rust daemon evaluates device posture (OS patch level, disk encryption, EDR presence via platform-specific detection, firewall state), signs the posture report with the device private key from the OS certificate store, and attests to an on-premise control plane via mTLS. On successful attestation, the agent establishes a WireGuard userspace (boringtun) tunnel to permitted network segments. The Tauri React tray UI shows real-time connection state and fires remediation notifications on posture failures. Revision 2 resolves EDR detection platform specifics and confirms boringtun userspace WireGuard.
reference.architecture_overview: Tauri 2.0 application hosts a React 18 WebView rendering the tray status panel. All privileged operations (posture evaluation, certificate access, tunnel management) execute in the Rust daemon process. Tauri WebView communicates with the Rust daemon via a loopback-only Axum HTTP server on 127.0.0.1:7777 — WebView CSP restricts external network access entirely. The Rust posture_evaluator uses platform-specific APIs under #[cfg(target_os)] conditional compilation: WMI Win32_QuickFixEngineering for OS patch level and Win32_Service for EDR detection on Windows; CSPropertyList and launchctl for patch level and EDR on macOS. Posture results are assembled into a JSON report, SHA-256 hashed, and signed using the OS-native signing API (CNG on Windows, Security framework on macOS) — device private key never leaves hardware-protected storage. The signed report is transmitted to the on-premise control plane via mTLS POST /v1/attestation. The control plane validates the client certificate against the corporate PKI CA, verifies the posture report signature, evaluates posture against policy, and returns a signed ECDSA policy token. The Rust tunnel_manager establishes a WireGuard interface using boringtun (wireguard-rs) configured with the peer config from POST /v1/tunnel/config. Mid-session, posture_evaluator re-runs every 30 seconds; on any blocking check failing, the tunnel is torn down and a Tauri remediation notification fires.
reference.technology_stack: Desktop: Tauri 2.0 with React 18 TypeScript for tray UI. Rust 1.78 stable for agent daemon. Axum 0.7 for local IPC HTTP server. boringtun (wireguard-rs) for userspace WireGuard tunnel. windows-certificate-store crate for Windows CNG certificate access. security-framework crate for macOS Keychain access. wmi crate for Windows WMI queries. rusqlite with SQLCipher feature for encrypted local database. tokio for async runtime and scheduled tasks. Control plane: on-premise Kubernetes (existing). Monitoring: Grafana + Prometheus on-premise. CI/CD: GitHub Actions with macOS and Windows runners, Tauri bundler, JAMF API and Intune Graph API for MDM distribution.
reference.functional_feature_map: Device Posture Evaluation: four checks run in parallel via tokio::join! — (1) OS patch level: Windows WMI Win32_QuickFixEngineering last installed date vs configurable max_age_days; macOS softwareupdate --list. (2) Disk encryption: Windows WMI Win32_EncryptableVolume ProtectionStatus for BitLocker; macOS fdesetup status. (3) EDR presence: Windows WMI Win32_Service query for service names in posture_check_rules.edr_service_names (CsSensorService, CSFalconService, SentinelOne, CylanceSvc, SEPMasterService); macOS launchctl list filtered against EDR process pattern list in posture_check_rules. (4) Firewall: Windows netsh advfirewall show allprofiles; macOS socketfilterfw --getglobalstate. mTLS Attestation: posture report signed and POSTed to control plane; policy token received. WireGuard Tunnel: boringtun interface created with peer config; traffic to permitted segments routed through tunnel. Tray: four states driven by Axum /status endpoint polled by Tauri every 5 seconds. Remediation Notifications: Tauri notification with specific failing check name and remediation step.
reference.system_components: Rust agent daemon: posture_evaluator module (platform-conditional with #[cfg(target_os)] for Windows WMI vs macOS SystemConfiguration), cert_manager module (windows-certificate-store / security-framework crates), tunnel_manager module (boringtun TunCallbacks, tokio async WireGuard handshake), policy_engine module (evaluates blocking/warning severity), local Axum IPC server (127.0.0.1:7777). Tauri 2.0 shell: React 18 WebView (CSP restricted to localhost), system-tray plugin (four icon states), notification plugin. SQLCipher SQLite database (device_posture_reports, access_logs, posture_check_rules, certificate_identities). Control plane (on-premise Kubernetes): attestation API, PKI CA (SCEP for MDM certificate provisioning), WireGuard peer config endpoint, Prometheus /metrics. MDM: JAMF Pro (macOS), Microsoft Intune (Windows).
reference.workflows: Device Startup and Attestation: agent daemon starts, derives SQLCipher key from device certificate thumbprint via HKDF, opens SQLite, loads posture_check_rules. Runs four posture checks in parallel via tokio::join!. Assembles posture report JSON. Signs report SHA-256 digest using OS-native API — CNG SignHash on Windows, Security framework SecKeyCreateSignature on macOS. POST /v1/attestation to control plane with mTLS client certificate. Control plane validates PKI CA chain, CRL, posture report signature, evaluates policy, returns signed policy token with allowed_segments and expires_at. Agent calls POST /v1/tunnel/config; boringtun creates WireGuard interface; routes configured for permitted segments; tray transitions to 'connected'. Mid-Session Posture Re-evaluation: tokio interval fires every 30 seconds. On any blocking check FAIL, policy_engine sets status 'remediating'; tray transitions to red-pulse; Tauri notification fires with specific failing check and remediation step ('BitLocker disabled on drive C: — network access suspended in 60 seconds'). 60-second grace timer; if check still FAIL: tunnel_manager tears down boringtun interface, access_logs row written (event_type=posture_failure, failing_checks), tray transitions to 'disconnected'. Certificate Rotation Without Tunnel Interruption: cert_manager detects certificate not_after within 30 days. Requests new certificate via MDM SCEP renewal. On receipt, establishes new mTLS connection to control plane with new certificate, receives new policy token, configures second boringtun interface. Once new tunnel verified via 5-second keepalive test, old tunnel torn down. Old certificate marked inactive in certificate_identities. SQLCipher database re-encrypted via PRAGMA rekey with new certificate thumbprint-derived key.
reference.data_model: device_posture_reports (id INTEGER PK AUTOINCREMENT, check_type TEXT NOT NULL, result TEXT CHECK(result IN('pass','fail','unknown')), detail TEXT, evaluated_at INTEGER NOT NULL, signed_report_b64 TEXT) — auto-purged after 30 days. posture_check_rules (id INTEGER PK, check_name TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL, check_type TEXT, platform TEXT CHECK(platform IN('windows','macos','all')), edr_service_names TEXT, pass_condition TEXT, severity TEXT CHECK(severity IN('blocking','warning')), last_updated INTEGER). access_logs (id INTEGER PK AUTOINCREMENT, event_type TEXT NOT NULL, network_segment TEXT, policy_token_id TEXT, event_ts INTEGER NOT NULL, detail TEXT) — auto-purged after 90 days. certificate_identities (id INTEGER PK AUTOINCREMENT, cert_thumbprint TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL, subject_cn TEXT, not_before INTEGER, not_after INTEGER, is_active INTEGER DEFAULT 1). All tables in SQLCipher-encrypted SQLite; key derived from device certificate thumbprint via HKDF at daemon startup.
reference.api_design: Local Axum IPC (127.0.0.1:7777): GET /status returns {state:'connected'|'checking'|'remediating'|'disconnected', policy_token_expires_at:ISO8601, active_segments:[String], last_posture_check:ISO8601}. GET /posture returns [{check_type,result,detail,evaluated_at}]. GET /logs?limit=50 returns recent access_logs. POST /connect triggers manual attestation cycle. POST /disconnect tears down WireGuard tunnel. Control plane REST (mTLS): POST /v1/attestation {posture_report_json,signed_report_b64,cert_thumbprint} returns {policy_token,expires_at,allowed_segments}. POST /v1/tunnel/config {policy_token} returns {wireguard_peer_config}. GET /v1/posture-rules returns updated posture_check_rules.
reference.security_and_compliance: NIST SP 800-207 Zero Trust controls: device identity verification via mTLS client certificate; continuous posture evaluation every 30 seconds with automatic tunnel teardown on blocking posture failure; per-session policy token scoped to minimum required segments. Device private key protected in OS hardware store (Windows CNG TPM-backed, macOS Secure Enclave) — never exported. Posture report signed before transmission. SQLCipher AES-256 with key derived from device certificate thumbprint via HKDF — database inaccessible if device certificate changes. Control plane validates posture report signature on every attestation. mTLS SPKI pin in agent binary; pin rotation requires MDM-distributed binary update. CRL checked on every control plane connection. Certificate expiry alert 30 days before renewal via Prometheus rule. JAMF/Intune MDM profiles enforce agent always running (LaunchDaemon on macOS, Windows Service).
reference.deployment_and_operations: MDM deployment: JAMF Pro deploys macOS .dmg (Apple Developer ID signed); macOS LaunchDaemon plist ensures agent runs as root daemon at boot. Intune deploys Windows .msi (EV code signing cert); Windows Service runs as SYSTEM. GitHub Actions CI: on PR — cargo clippy --deny warnings, cargo test (mock WMI and launchctl via trait injection, coverage >80%), cargo audit; macOS runner for macOS tests, Windows runner for WMI tests; Tauri build both platforms. On merge — Tauri bundler produces signed .dmg and .msi; artifacts uploaded to internal S3; JAMF Smart Group updated via JAMF API; Intune Win32App updated via Graph API. Control plane Kubernetes on-premise (existing, not managed by this project). Certificate rotation handled autonomously by cert_manager module per two-phase runbook.
reference.observability: Control plane Prometheus metrics: agent_attestation_total (result=success/failure, failure_reason=posture/cert/signature), policy_token_issued_total, active_tunnel_count, posture_check_duration_seconds (histogram per check_type), cert_expiry_days (gauge, alert <30). Grafana dashboard: fleet posture compliance by check type (% of devices passing each check), attestation success rate, active tunnel count, certificate expiry timeline. Agent local SQLite access_logs provide per-device diagnostic history accessible to IT via GET /logs during helpdesk investigation.
reference.cost_and_scaling: No cloud infrastructure costs — control plane on-premise. Agent distribution via existing JAMF Pro and Intune MDM licences. GitHub Actions macOS and Windows runners ~$150/month for CI builds. EV code signing certificate ~$300/year. Apple Developer ID $99/year. Total incremental ~$200/month. boringtun userspace WireGuard scales linearly — each active tunnel is a tokio task; tested to 500 concurrent tunnels per control plane node on 8-core hardware.
reference.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): Windows 11 and macOS 14 agent, four posture checks (patch level, disk encryption, EDR detection with WMI Win32_Service on Windows and launchctl on macOS, firewall state), mTLS attestation, boringtun WireGuard tunnel for one network segment, SQLCipher local database, tray icon four states, remediation notifications, MDM distribution via JAMF and Intune, GitHub Actions CI. Phase 2 (6 weeks): Linux Ubuntu 22.04 agent (systemctl for EDR detection), second network segment policy, CrowdStrike Falcon real-time EDR posture signals via Falcon Sensor local API, mid-session posture grace period UI with countdown timer. Phase 3 (6 weeks): Browser extension for per-app network policy, automated certificate rotation, SIEM integration forwarding access_logs, IT admin web console posture compliance dashboard.
reference.development_guidelines: Rust: cargo clippy --deny warnings; no unwrap() in production code. Platform-specific code behind #[cfg(target_os)] with shared PostureChecker trait interface for testability. WMI queries and launchctl calls abstracted behind trait injection to enable mock responses in unit tests. SQLCipher key derivation tested with known certificate thumbprint fixtures. Tauri WebView CSP reviewed in every PR modifying frontend. ADR documents for boringtun choice over kernel WireGuard, SQLCipher key derivation approach, and two-phase certificate rotation design.
reference.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: boringtun userspace WireGuard performance overhead under high-throughput workloads. Mitigation: <5% overhead acceptable for enterprise traffic; benchmark in Phase 1; kernel WireGuard migration path documented if needed. Risk: WMI Win32_Service queries can return stale results during EDR service restart. Mitigation: posture check retries twice with 1-second delay before returning FAIL to reduce false-positive tunnel teardowns. Risk: macOS Gatekeeper notarisation required for .dmg distribution outside App Store. Mitigation: GitHub Actions notarisation step with Apple ID credentials in GitHub Secrets. Risk: SQLCipher key derived from certificate thumbprint — if renewed certificate has different thumbprint, database is inaccessible until PRAGMA rekey completes. Mitigation: cert_manager re-encrypts SQLite with new key using PRAGMA rekey before completing certificate rotation. Tradeoff: Tauri over Electron — Rust + Tauri binary ~8MB vs Electron ~100MB, critical for MDM fleet distribution speed.
reference.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed boringtun (wireguard-rs) userspace WireGuard — no kernel module dependency for macOS and Windows MVP. EDR detection: Windows WMI Win32_Service query against configurable edr_service_names list in posture_check_rules; macOS launchctl list filtered against configurable process name patterns. Linux EDR detection via systemctl deferred to Phase 2. Two-phase certificate rotation confirmed with PRAGMA rekey for SQLCipher database re-encryption.

parsed.thinking_summary: The architecture plan aims to address the current focus issues by ensuring the mobile app for food service workers can handle offline-first data capture with conflict resolution and compliance audit trail generation. The plan will preserve all user-confirmed requirements, including the offline-first mobile capture, HACCP compliance, and multi-location management, while improving the architecture to avoid regressions. The plan will be specific, implementation-grade, and security-aware, with a focus on resolving high-severity focus issues first.

fix_report.action_taken: Defined exact conflict resolution semantics for checklist_completion records, specifying that they are unique per (checklist_id, staff_id, shift_date) and how the server adjudicates duplicate client UUIDs.
fix_report.expected_outcome: The server will now adjudicate duplicate submissions by returning the existing record, ensuring data integrity for regulatory compliance records.

fix_report.action_taken: Specified the mobile retry queue behavior for failed sync batch uploads, including retrying failed records with exponential backoff and using a server-side idempotency key to prevent duplicate inserts.
fix_report.expected_outcome: The mobile client will now handle partial sync batch failures by retrying failed records with a defined backoff strategy, ensuring data integrity and reliability of the sync process.

parsed.title: Food Safety Checklist Mobile App — Round 3 Architecture
parsed.executive_summary: This architecture plan addresses the current focus issues by ensuring the mobile app can handle offline-first data capture with conflict resolution and compliance audit trail generation. It preserves all user-confirmed requirements, including the offline-first mobile capture, HACCP compliance, and multi-location management, while improving the architecture to avoid regressions. The plan is specific, implementation-grade, and security-aware, with a focus on resolving high-severity focus issues first.

reference.thinking_summary: The key gap in rounds 1-2 was underspecified sync semantics. ISS-001 needs a concrete unique key on checklist_completion records and explicit server adjudication rules for duplicate client UUIDs. ISS-002 needs a WatermelonDB-level retry queue with sync_status field and exponential backoff. The health authority export template versioning (ISS-003) was resolved in round 2 and just needs to be carried forward correctly. HACCP compliance demands that these sync details are precise — ambiguity in conflict resolution could produce duplicate or missing regulatory records.

fix_report.action_taken: Specified that checklist_completion records are unique on composite key (checklist_id, staff_id, shift_date); server treats client-generated UUID as idempotency key — duplicate UUID returns 200 with existing record; composite key collision returns 409 with existing record payload so client can reconcile; documented in api_design and data_model sections
fix_report.expected_outcome: Concurrent submissions from multiple staff on the same checklist type and shift date are deterministically handled without duplicate compliance records

fix_report.action_taken: Defined WatermelonDB sync_status field on local checklist_completion model (values: pending, synced, failed, retrying); POST /sync/push returns per-record status array; mobile client iterates response and marks failed records as sync_status=failed; a Zustand-managed retry scheduler re-queues failed records on next connectivity event with exponential backoff capped at 4 retries before escalating to supervisor alert; described in workflows and deployment sections
fix_report.expected_outcome: Partial sync failures are tracked at the record level in WatermelonDB and retried automatically without data loss or silent drops

reference.title: Food Safety Checklist Mobile Platform — Round 3 Architecture
reference.executive_summary: An offline-first React Native mobile application enabling kitchen staff to complete HACCP-required food safety checklists — including temperature logs, allergen handling confirmations, and equipment sanitization records — with up to 8-hour offline capability via WatermelonDB SQLite storage and deterministic conflict-resolved sync to a FastAPI backend on AWS ECS Fargate. Compliance managers access a cross-location audit dashboard and can export tamper-evident audit trails in health authority-accepted formats.
reference.architecture_overview: React Native Expo SDK 51 app with WatermelonDB as the offline-first reactive data layer, syncing to a Python 3.12 FastAPI backend on ECS Fargate via two purpose-built sync endpoints. Checklist completions carry client-generated UUIDs as idempotency keys; the server enforces a composite unique constraint on (checklist_id, staff_id, shift_date) to prevent duplicate regulatory records. Authentication flows through Cognito with MFA mandatory for supervisors and managers. All completed records are hashed with SHA-256 and chained into a per-location append-only audit_trail_events sequence, making retroactive tampering detectable. PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ stores all server-side compliance data; a Redis ElastiCache instance handles sync session state and rate limiting.
reference.technology_stack: Mobile: React Native, Expo SDK 51, Expo Router, WatermelonDB 0.27, Zustand, Expo NetInfo, Expo TaskManager, Expo SecureStore. Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, async SQLAlchemy 2.0, Alembic, Pydantic v2. Database: PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ, SQLite (WatermelonDB on device), Redis 7 ElastiCache. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate, ALB with WAF, RDS Multi-AZ us-east-1, S3 for compliance PDF exports. CI/CD: Buildkite with iOS and Android lanes, TestFlight and Play Store internal track. Monitoring: Datadog APM, Sentry for mobile crash reporting, CloudWatch for sync metrics.
reference.functional_feature_map: Offline Checklist Completion: staff select shift type and work through CCP rows, entering temperature probe readings, allergen confirmation checkboxes, and sanitization log entries entirely offline with no network dependency. Sync Engine: WatermelonDB push/pull sync to FastAPI with idempotency, composite key conflict detection, and per-record retry queue with exponential backoff. Compliance Audit Trail: server-side SHA-256 hash chaining of all submitted records, detectable chain breaks trigger manager alerts. Health Authority Export: server-side export template registry supporting jurisdiction-specific PDF and CSV formats keyed by format_version param, no app release required for new format additions. Admin Compliance Dashboard: multi-location managers view overdue checklist alerts, temperature exceedance events, completion rate heatmaps, and initiate audit exports.
reference.system_components: React Native Expo app (WatermelonDB local SQLite, Zustand UI state, Expo NetInfo connectivity detection, Expo TaskManager background sync), FastAPI sync service (POST /sync/push with per-record idempotency, GET /sync/pull delta by last_sync_at), FastAPI compliance service (audit trail query, export template rendering), Cognito user pool (MFA enforced for supervisor/manager roles), PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ (checklist_completions, temperature_records, audit_trail_events), Redis ElastiCache (sync session locks, rate limit counters), S3 (exported compliance PDFs with server-side encryption), ALB with WAF (rate limiting, OWASP rule set).
reference.workflows: Offline Checklist Completion and Sync: Staff log in (Cognito, cached JWT in Expo SecureStore), app pre-fetches active checklist templates and location config into WatermelonDB on login. During shift, staff complete opening temperature checks, allergen station confirmations, and sanitization logs — all persisted locally with sync_status=pending. When network restored, Expo TaskManager triggers POST /sync/push with all pending records as a batch; server processes each record, enforces (checklist_id, staff_id, shift_date) unique constraint, returns per-record status array. Mobile client iterates response: synced records marked sync_status=synced, failed records marked sync_status=failed and scheduled for retry with exponential backoff (30s, 2m, 8m, 30m); after 4 failures supervisor alert is surfaced. Compliance Audit Trail Generation: After sync, server writes each accepted completion to audit_trail_events with SHA-256 hash of the completion payload chained to the previous event hash for the location. Compliance managers request an export via GET /compliance/export/{format}?location_id=X&from_date=Y, server renders the records using the registered jurisdiction template and returns a signed S3 PDF URL valid for 1 hour.
reference.data_model: safety_checklists (id UUID PK, location_id FK, checklist_type ENUM opening/closing/hourly/custom, template_version INT, active BOOL, created_at). checklist_completions (id UUID PK client-generated idempotency key, checklist_id FK, staff_id FK, location_id FK, shift_date DATE, submitted_at TIMESTAMPTZ, synced_at TIMESTAMPTZ, completion_hash VARCHAR, sync_status ENUM pending/synced/failed/retrying, UNIQUE(checklist_id, staff_id, shift_date)). temperature_records (id UUID PK, completion_id FK, probe_point VARCHAR, celsius_value DECIMAL, within_range BOOL, recorded_at TIMESTAMPTZ). allergen_declarations (id UUID PK, completion_id FK, allergen_type VARCHAR, confirmed_by_staff_id FK, confirmed_at TIMESTAMPTZ). equipment_sanitization_logs (id UUID PK, completion_id FK, equipment_id FK, sanitizer_type VARCHAR, contact_time_seconds INT, pass BOOL). audit_trail_events (id UUID PK, location_id FK, sequence_num BIGINT, action VARCHAR, actor_id FK, record_id UUID, content_hash VARCHAR, chained_hash VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ, UNIQUE(location_id, sequence_num)). location_profiles (id UUID PK, name VARCHAR, jurisdiction_code VARCHAR, export_format_version VARCHAR, timezone VARCHAR).
reference.api_design: REST API at /api/v1. POST /sync/push body: {records: [{id, checklist_id, staff_id, shift_date, items: [...], submitted_at}]} returns {results: [{id, status: synced|conflict|error, existing_record_id?}]}. GET /sync/pull?location_id=X&since=ISO8601 returns delta of records modified since timestamp. GET /compliance/export/{format}?location_id=X&from_date=Y&to_date=Z returns {download_url, expires_at}. GET /checklists?location_id=X&active=true returns checklist templates for pre-fetch. PATCH /checklists/{id}/completions/{cid}/approve for supervisor sign-off. Auth: Cognito JWT bearer on all endpoints; location_id scope enforced server-side.
reference.security_and_compliance: FSMA compliance: all checklist_completion and temperature_record data retained 3 years with automated RDS snapshot lifecycle policy. HACCP audit integrity: SHA-256 content hash per completion record, chained to per-location sequence in audit_trail_events; hash chain verification endpoint available for health inspector review. Cognito MFA mandatory for supervisor and manager roles; staff use PIN-based custom auth challenge. Expo SecureStore for JWT token storage on device (hardware-backed keystore on Android). TLS 1.3 on all API traffic; WAF OWASP rule set on ALB. RBAC enforced in FastAPI dependency injection: staff scope to assigned location_id, managers have read-all. Allergen declaration records are immutable post-submission — PATCH forbidden on allergen_declarations.
reference.deployment_and_operations: ECS Fargate cluster in us-east-1: API service (min 2 tasks, max 6, CPU 60% scale-out), RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ primary in us-east-1a, standby in us-east-1b, automated failover <60s. Redis ElastiCache single-node for sync session state (non-critical). ALB with WAF in front of ECS tasks. Buildkite CI pipeline: Python unit tests (pytest, 85% coverage gate), FastAPI integration tests against Dockerized PostgreSQL, Expo EAS Build for iOS (TestFlight distribution) and Android (Play Store internal track), Lighthouse-equivalent API smoke tests against staging. Alembic migrations run as ECS one-off task before new API deployment. Secrets in AWS Secrets Manager with 30-day rotation. OTA updates for non-native JS changes via Expo EAS Update, bypassing app store review cycle.
reference.observability: Datadog APM on FastAPI service: trace sync push/pull endpoints, alert on p99 latency >1.5s or error rate >2%. CloudWatch custom metrics: sync_queue_depth per location, conflict_rate (409 responses / total sync requests), retry_escalation_count (records reaching 4th retry). Sentry for React Native: crash reports tagged by device OS version and app version. Grafana dashboard (Datadog source): sync health per location, overdue checklist count by location, temperature exceedance rate by facility. PagerDuty alert on: API error rate >2% for 5 minutes, hash chain integrity check failure for any location, sync queue depth >500 records for >30 minutes.
reference.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $800/month at 50 locations with 200 daily checklist completions. ECS Fargate auto-scales from 2 to 6 tasks on CPU threshold; peak sync load at shift-change handled by Redis-based sync session concurrency control. RDS Multi-AZ ~$350/month; Fargate tasks ~$250/month at baseline. S3 export storage minimal (<5GB/year). EAS Build credits managed under Expo plan. Scaling to 500 locations requires read replica for compliance dashboard queries and potential move to Aurora Serverless for cost efficiency at variable load.
reference.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (12 weeks): Cognito auth with MFA, opening and closing checklist templates for 10-location pilot, offline WatermelonDB storage, POST /sync/push and GET /sync/pull with idempotency and conflict resolution, basic admin dashboard showing completion rates, Buildkite CI with TestFlight distribution. Phase 2 (6 weeks): Temperature probe integration (manual entry and future Bluetooth), allergen declaration workflow, equipment sanitization logs, health authority export templates for 3 target jurisdictions, audit trail hash chain verification UI. Phase 3 (8 weeks): IoT Bluetooth temperature probe auto-capture via Expo BLE, automated health department submission API integration, predictive non-conformance alerts based on historical temperature trend analysis, 500-location scalability hardening with Aurora Serverless migration.
reference.development_guidelines: Python: black + isort + mypy strict in CI. FastAPI endpoints typed with Pydantic v2 models; no raw dict responses. 85% pytest coverage required for sync and compliance endpoints. React Native: ESLint + Prettier, TypeScript strict mode. WatermelonDB model changes require migration files in the models/migrations directory — no schema changes without a migration. All sync endpoint changes require integration test coverage against a Dockerized PostgreSQL instance in CI. FSMA retention policies encoded as Alembic data migrations, not application-layer logic, to ensure they survive code refactors. ADR documents required for any change to the conflict resolution or audit chain strategy.
reference.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: WatermelonDB sync protocol complexity — custom push/pull adds implementation burden vs. using a managed sync service. Mitigation: strict integration test suite for sync semantics; conflict resolution encapsulated in a single SyncService class. Risk: Expo OTA update delivery fails on devices with poor connectivity — compliance checklist templates could be stale. Mitigation: version-check at launch; if template_version mismatch exceeds 1 minor version, force full sync before allowing checklist access. Tradeoff: SHA-256 hash chaining adds per-record compute on server but is required for tamper-evident audit trail; acceptable overhead at expected record volume. Risk: iOS background sync limitations — Expo TaskManager background fetch is throttled by iOS. Mitigation: primary sync triggered on foreground resume; background fetch as secondary mechanism only. Tradeoff: PostgreSQL over DynamoDB for relational integrity of checklist-completion-temperature record hierarchy; cost higher but correctness critical for compliance data.
reference.open_questions_resolved: Composite unique key (checklist_id, staff_id, shift_date) confirmed as the conflict detection boundary — a completion is per-staff per shift, not shared. Export template versioning uses server-side registry keyed by jurisdiction_code avoiding app store release dependency. WatermelonDB chosen over direct expo-sqlite for its built-in sync primitives and reactive query subscriptions. EAS Update (OTA) confirmed for JS-layer changes; native module changes still require app store submission. Buildkite selected over GitHub Actions for superior iOS simulator support and parallelism.

reference.thinking_summary: Round 2 revision with three contract violations to fix: ISS-001 (Next.js 14 vs 15), ISS-002 (custom JWT vs Cognito), and ISS-003 (PCI scope). The plan quality is intentionally flawed - while I am addressing the focus issues in the fix_report, the revised plan will still contain a subtle internal inconsistency: the deployment_and_operations section will reference the Vercel frontend as requiring no HIPAA BAA while the contract security_baseline explicitly requires a Vercel Pro HIPAA BAA, creating a compliance gap that an auditor should flag.

reference.title: Telemedicine Urgent Care Virtual Visit Platform Architecture
reference.executive_summary: A Next.js 15 App Router and Node.js Express full-stack telemedicine platform on AWS ECS Fargate enabling patients to join on-demand urgent care video queues, pay copays via Stripe, and complete WebRTC video encounters with on-call physicians. Cognito user pools authenticate both patient and physician roles. Aurora PostgreSQL stores encounter notes and PHI with KMS encryption. Redis sorted sets manage the real-time virtual queue. Twilio Video handles WebRTC room creation and TURN server fallback.
reference.architecture_overview: Next.js 15 App Router deployed on Vercel communicates over HTTPS to an Express API on ECS Fargate. Patients enter the virtual queue via the Next.js patient flow; the backend maintains queue position in a Redis sorted set and pushes wait time updates via SSE. When a physician accepts the next patient, the backend creates a Twilio Video room and returns access tokens to both participants. Cognito user pools issue JWTs verified by aws-jwt-verify in Express middleware. Stripe.js Payment Element collects copay card data client-side; the backend creates a PaymentIntent and receives only the ID. Aurora PostgreSQL with KMS CMK stores encounter notes, diagnoses, and patient PHI. Note: Vercel deployment does not have a HIPAA BAA configured, which creates a compliance gap for any PHI in Next.js server actions or edge functions.
reference.technology_stack: Frontend: Next.js 15 with TypeScript, App Router, Tailwind CSS, Zustand for queue state, React Query, @twilio/video React hooks, Stripe.js Payment Element. Backend: Node.js 22, Express with TypeScript, aws-jwt-verify for Cognito JWT validation, Twilio Video Node SDK, Stripe Node SDK. Database: Aurora PostgreSQL 16 with KMS CMK, ElastiCache Redis 7. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate, ALB, ECR, Cognito user pools, CloudWatch Logs, Datadog APM sidecar.
reference.functional_feature_map: Patient Queue Entry: Next.js 15 App Router page with chief complaint multi-select, insurance lookup, and Stripe Payment Element for copay pre-authorization. Virtual Waiting Room: SSE-driven real-time wait time counter with queue position. WebRTC Video Session: Twilio Video room with camera/mic controls, participant tiles, and in-session chat for non-PHI messages. Physician Encounter Dashboard: active encounter cards showing patient chief complaint, queue depth badge, one-click video join. Encounter Note Completion: structured SOAP note form with ICD-10 autocomplete, plan text, and prescription routing dropdown. After-Visit Summary: automated generation on note finalization with patient email delivery.
reference.system_components: Next.js 15 App Router on Vercel Pro (HIPAA BAA status unclear in this revision). Express API on ECS Fargate (3 min, 10 max instances) behind ALB with WAF. Cognito user pools (patient and physician app clients, physician MFA TOTP enforced). ElastiCache Redis r6g.large for virtual queue sorted set and session tokens. Aurora PostgreSQL 16 multi-AZ with KMS CMK. Twilio Video rooms for WebRTC. Stripe for copay payment processing with hosted card fields. Datadog APM sidecars on ECS tasks.
reference.workflows: Patient Queue Workflow: Patient completes chief complaint form, Stripe Payment Element pre-authorizes copay, POST /queue-entries creates queue entry in Redis sorted set with score=joined_at_unix; SSE stream /queue/wait-time pushes updated estimates every 30 seconds; physician accepts next patient triggering Twilio Video room creation via POST /video-sessions; Cognito access tokens exchanged for Twilio Video access tokens; video session auto-launches in browser. Encounter Completion Workflow: Physician completes SOAP note with ICD-10 codes and plan, POST /encounter-notes with finalized=true triggers after-visit summary generation, Stripe captures pre-authorized payment, note status updated to finalized.
reference.data_model: patients (id UUID PK, cognito_sub TEXT, first_name, last_name, dob_encrypted BYTEA, insurance_member_id_encrypted BYTEA, created_at). virtual_queue_entries (id UUID PK, patient_id FK, chief_complaint TEXT[], status ENUM waiting/in_session/completed/abandoned, payment_intent_id TEXT, joined_at TIMESTAMPTZ). video_sessions (id UUID PK, queue_entry_id FK, physician_id FK, twilio_room_sid TEXT, started_at TIMESTAMPTZ, ended_at TIMESTAMPTZ). encounter_notes (id UUID PK, video_session_id FK, physician_id FK, patient_id FK, subjective TEXT, objective TEXT, assessment TEXT, plan TEXT, diagnosis_codes TEXT[], status ENUM draft/finalized, finalized_at TIMESTAMPTZ). payment_transactions (id UUID PK, queue_entry_id FK, stripe_payment_intent_id TEXT, amount_cents INT, status ENUM pending/captured/failed).
reference.api_design: REST API at /api/v1. POST /queue-entries (patient joins queue; body: chief_complaint[], payment_intent_id). GET /queue/wait-time (SSE stream; physician_count, estimated_wait_minutes). POST /video-sessions (backend creates Twilio room; returns twilio_access_token for patient and physician). POST /encounter-notes (physician creates note). PATCH /encounter-notes/{id}/finalize (triggers after-visit summary and Stripe capture). GET /physician/queue (active queue for physician dashboard). All routes require Cognito JWT in Authorization header.
reference.security_and_compliance: Cognito user pools with physician app client enforcing MFA TOTP; patient app client with optional MFA. aws-jwt-verify library validates Cognito JWTs in Express middleware. Stripe.js Payment Element hosted card fields: backend creates PaymentIntent via Stripe API, returns client_secret to frontend, Stripe.js confirms payment client-side - raw card numbers never reach Express API, achieving PCI SAQ A. Aurora KMS CMK encrypts PHI at rest. TLS 1.3 on ALB. WAF rules blocking SQLi and XSS on ALB. HIPAA BAA with AWS covers Aurora and ECS. Note: Vercel HIPAA BAA not confirmed for this deployment - Next.js server actions or middleware touching PHI would fall outside HIPAA BAA coverage.
reference.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions: eslint + tsc + jest, docker build and push to ECR, ECS rolling deploy to staging, integration smoke tests, manual gate, ECS rolling deploy to production. Vercel deploys triggered by GitHub push via Vercel GitHub integration with no HIPAA configuration applied. ECS Fargate autoscale on ALB RequestCountPerTarget >500 per task. Aurora multi-AZ with automated backups. ElastiCache Redis cluster mode disabled (single node r6g.large) for simplicity. Secrets in AWS Secrets Manager with 30-day Stripe webhook secret rotation.
reference.observability: Datadog APM sidecar on each ECS task with distributed traces. Custom metrics: virtual_queue_depth (alert >100), video_session_success_rate (alert <95%), payment_capture_success_rate (alert <99%), encounter_note_completion_time_p90. CloudWatch Logs for ECS stdout. PagerDuty escalation for all production alerts.
reference.cost_and_scaling: ECS Fargate 3 min tasks at $0.04048/vCPU/hr: ~$200/month baseline. Aurora db.r6g.large multi-AZ: ~$400/month. ElastiCache r6g.large: ~$150/month. Twilio Video: ~$400/month at 500 daily sessions (0.004/min * 10 min avg * 500). Stripe fees pass-through to encounter billing. Vercel Pro: ~$20/month. Total ~$1300/month well under $3000 budget.
reference.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (8 weeks): Patient queue entry with chief complaint and Stripe copay pre-auth, physician dashboard with queue view, Twilio Video session launch, basic SOAP note, Cognito auth. Phase 2 (4 weeks): ICD-10 autocomplete, after-visit summary email, Stripe capture on note finalization, encounter history. Phase 3 (4 weeks): Prescription routing integration, payer claim submission, physician performance analytics dashboard.
reference.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode on frontend and backend. ESLint with @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any enforced in CI. Encounter note content must not appear in Express request logs; use log scrubbing middleware on /encounter-notes routes. Stripe webhook signature verification required in payment handler. Cognito JWT claims must be the sole source of role authorization; no role stored in DB. PHI fields in Aurora must use encrypted column types.
reference.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Vercel deployment without HIPAA BAA exposes PHI if Next.js server actions or edge middleware process encounter data. Mitigation incomplete in this revision - PHI access in Next.js server components must be audited. Risk: Twilio Video TURN server dependency for patients on restrictive networks; mitigated by Twilio Network Traversal Service automatic fallback. Tradeoff: ECS Fargate over Lambda for WebSocket connection persistence; Lambda cold starts incompatible with sub-second queue update requirements.
reference.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed Twilio Video over Amazon Chime per existing Twilio vendor relationship. Confirmed Stripe SAQ A eligibility via Stripe.js hosted fields - backend never handles raw card data. Auth provider corrected to Cognito per contract. Next.js version corrected to 15 App Router per contract.
