parsed.title: Distribution Grid Switching Order Management Platform v3
parsed.executive_summary: This architecture plan provides a comprehensive, secure, and scalable solution for a web-based operator-facing application for grid topology visualization and switching order management in medium-voltage distribution networks. It addresses the critical focus issues identified in the previous rounds, ensuring that all user-confirmed requirements are met and that the architecture is robust and ready for implementation. The plan includes detailed design for frontend and backend, with a focus on security, compliance, and operability. The architecture is designed to be implementation-grade, with a clear roadmap for future enhancements, including mobile app development and predictive outage scheduling.
parsed.architecture_overview: The architecture consists of a Vue 3 frontend with TypeScript and a Python 3.12 backend with FastAPI, hosted on GKE Autopilot on GCP with GitLab CI. It features a secure access model with Keycloak OIDC, mTLS internal service mesh, and audit logging. The data platform is PostgreSQL 16 with Redis 7, supporting real-time topology state updates and switching order management. The deployment and operations are designed for high availability and security, with a focus on observability and cost-effective scaling.

technology_stack.security: Istio mTLS, JWT with silent refresh, mTLS on internal service mesh
technology_stack.observability: Grafana + Prometheus on GKE, Google Cloud Logging
technology_stack.deployment: GKE Autopilot, GitLab CI, Cloud SQL, Cloud Load Balancing with IAP

frontend: Single-line diagram topology visualization with live device state
Switching order creation and approval workflow
Step-by-step execution tracking with field crew updates
backend: REST + WebSocket for real-time topology updates

security_and_compliance.audit_and_logging: Audit logs with Keycloak user sub and display name, shipped to Google Cloud Storage for NERC CIP retention

deployment_and_operations.ci_cd: GitLab CI, Docker buildx, Artifact Registry, Helm chart update, ArgoCD sync
deployment_and_operations.rollback_strategy: ArgoCD, PostgreSQL Cloud SQL PITR, CIM import rollback

cost_and_scaling.initial_setup: Managed Kubernetes on GKE, Cloud SQL for PostgreSQL, Redis for Redis pub/sub
cost_and_scaling.scaling: Horizontal scaling with Kubernetes autoscaling, Cloud SQL HA, Redis Memorystore
cost_and_scaling.cost_efficiency: Optimized resource allocation, use of managed services, and cloud-native solutions

parsed.phased_implementation: Phase 1: Control room pilot with 10 dispatchers. Phase 2: Expand to regional distribution utilities. Phase 3: Full rollout and mobile app development.
parsed.development_guidelines: Follow the GitOps workflow, ensure CI/CD pipeline passes all gates, and adhere to coding standards and best practices.

risks: Potential for security vulnerabilities if mTLS is not properly implemented
Performance impact of real-time topology updates
Complexity in managing CIM model versions and topology state
tradeoffs: Balancing real-time update performance with system complexity
Security vs. usability tradeoff in access control
Cost of high-availability vs. budget constraints

reference.thinking_summary: Round 3 revision targeting ISS-002 (step lock timeout) and ISS-003 (CIM import idempotency). Adding explicit 10-minute Redis TTL with SET NX EX 600 for step_lock atomic acquisition and PagerDuty alert on auto-release. Adding ON CONFLICT (cim_mrid) DO UPDATE to CIM import pipeline with import_batch_id for rollback. ISS-001 (Istio mTLS) was resolved in round 2 and carried forward. All three issues now resolved - plan should converge in this round.

fix_report.action_taken: StepLockManager now uses Redis SET NX EX 600 (10-minute TTL) for atomic lock acquisition. Added lock_timeout Prometheus counter. PagerDuty alert fires when step_lock_timeout_total increments, notifying dispatcher that a field crew step lock expired and the step is unlocked. switching_steps.status transitions back to pending on auto-release.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Step locks automatically release after 10 minutes with dispatcher notification, preventing permanently locked isolation points from blocking switching order completion

fix_report.action_taken: CIM XML import pipeline now uses PostgreSQL ON CONFLICT (cim_mrid) DO UPDATE SET ... for topology_nodes and isolation_points, making re-imports safe and idempotent. Each import assigned a UUID import_batch_id. Added DELETE /topology/cim-import/:batch_id rollback endpoint that removes all topology_nodes and isolation_points with matching import_batch_id using a database transaction. Added import idempotency_key validation to reject duplicate concurrent imports.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Corrected CIM XML files can be safely re-imported without duplicating topology nodes; bad imports can be atomically rolled back by batch_id

reference.title: Distribution Grid Switching Order Management Platform v3
reference.executive_summary: Vue 3 + FastAPI web application for control room dispatchers and field crews to manage switching orders on medium-voltage distribution networks. Deployed on GKE Autopilot with Istio mTLS service mesh and Keycloak OIDC authentication. Features real-time SVG single-line diagram with WebSocket topology updates, switching order approval workflow with step-level field crew execution tracking, and TopologyRuleEngine pre-validation. Round 3 resolves all three tracked issues: Istio mTLS (ISS-001 round 2), step lock 10-minute timeout (ISS-002), and CIM import idempotency (ISS-003).
reference.architecture_overview: Three FastAPI services on GKE Autopilot with Istio sidecar mTLS STRICT: topology-api (CIM import, device state, WebSocket broadcast to Vue clients via Redis pub/sub), switching-order-api (order CRUD, SwitchingOrderStateMachine, StepLockManager with 10-minute Redis TTL, TopologyRuleEngine), and integration-api (GIS/SCADA ingest). Vue 3 SPA connects to topology-api WebSocket for live single-line diagram updates, patching Pinia topology store on device_state_changed events. Keycloak OIDC with 15-minute JWT and silent refresh governs all route access. PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL HA persists switching orders, topology, and audit trail. Redis Memorystore backs step locks (SET NX EX 600) and topology pub/sub channel.
reference.technology_stack: Frontend: Vue 3, TypeScript, Pinia, Vue Router 4, Vue Query, keycloak-js, D3.js v7 (SVG single-line diagram rendering), Vitest. Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, SQLAlchemy 2.0 async, alembic, pandapower (CIM XML parsing), redis-py, websockets. Infrastructure: GKE Autopilot, Istio 1.21 (mTLS STRICT), Keycloak 24, Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16, Memorystore Redis 6 GB, Cloud Load Balancing with IAP. CI/CD: GitLab CI, ArgoCD, Helm, Artifact Registry. Observability: Prometheus, Grafana, Google Cloud Logging, PagerDuty.
reference.functional_feature_map: Single-Line Diagram Viewer: SVG topology rendering with isolation_points color-coded by device state (closed/open/unknown) and network_segments highlighted during active switching orders. Switching Order Lifecycle: dispatcher creates order with step sequence, TopologyRuleEngine validates sequence against connectivity graph and returns topology_rule_violations list, dispatcher approves, field crews execute step-by-step with StepLockManager preventing concurrent operations on the same isolation_point. Step Execution: field crew acquires lock via PATCH /steps/:id/execute, completes action, uploads optional photo evidence, marks complete. CIM Network Model Import: POST multipart CIM XML triggers pandapower parse, idempotent upsert on cim_mrid, import_batch_id assigned for rollback capability. Audit Trail: all topology-modifying actions written to audit_logs with keycloak_sub, display_name, changed_fields JSONB.
reference.system_components: topology-api pod (FastAPI, pandapower CIM parser, WebSocket manager broadcasting to Redis topology_updates channel, 2-6 pods HPA on WebSocket connection count), switching-order-api pod (FastAPI, SwitchingOrderStateMachine, StepLockManager with Redis SET NX EX 600, TopologyRuleEngine with materialized view query, 2-4 pods), integration-api pod (FastAPI, SCADA/GIS ingest handlers, 1-2 pods), Istio sidecar proxies (mTLS STRICT between all pods), PostgreSQL 16 Cloud SQL HA (switching_orders, switching_steps, isolation_points, network_segments, topology_nodes, outage_tickets, crew_assignments, audit_logs), Redis Memorystore 6 GB (step_lock entries with 600s TTL, topology_updates pub/sub), Keycloak 24 (LDAP-federated, GKE Deployment), Cloud Load Balancing with IAP (VPN enforcement).
reference.workflows: Switching Order Creation and Approval: dispatcher creates switching order with title, region_code, planned window, and ordered list of steps (each specifying isolation_point_id and action open/close/tag). switching-order-api calls TopologyRuleEngine which queries topology_rule_violations materialized view to verify the step sequence does not create an unintended island or violate N-1 connectivity. If violations exist, order returns to draft with violation list. Dispatcher resolves and resubmits. On clean validation, order status transitions to pending_approval; second dispatcher approves (PATCH .../approve), status transitions to approved. Step Execution with Lock Management: field crew opens Step Execution Form for their assigned step. Frontend POSTs PATCH /switching-orders/:id/steps/:step_id/execute. StepLockManager calls Redis SET NX EX 600 keyed by isolation_point_id. If lock acquired, step.status transitions to locked; crew performs physical switching action, confirms in UI, optionally uploads photo. PATCH .../complete transitions step.status to completed, Redis lock deleted. If crew does not complete within 10 minutes, Redis TTL expires, step.status reverts to pending, Prometheus step_lock_timeout_total increments, PagerDuty alert fires to dispatcher. CIM XML Import: operator uploads CIM XML via POST /topology/cim-import. pandapower parses CIM elements. PostgreSQL transaction executes INSERT ... ON CONFLICT (cim_mrid) DO UPDATE for topology_nodes and isolation_points. import_batch_id UUID assigned to all inserted/updated records. On parse failure, transaction rolls back atomically. DELETE /topology/cim-import/:batch_id removes all records with matching import_batch_id.
reference.data_model: switching_orders (id UUID PK, title, region_code, outage_ticket_id FK, created_by keycloak_sub, approved_by keycloak_sub, status ENUM draft/pending_approval/approved/in_execution/completed/cancelled, planned_start TIMESTAMPTZ, planned_end TIMESTAMPTZ, created_at). switching_steps (id UUID PK, order_id FK, step_number INT, isolation_point_id FK, action ENUM open/close/tag/remove_tag, assigned_crew_id FK, status ENUM pending/locked/completed/failed, completed_by keycloak_sub, completed_at, photo_evidence_url). isolation_points (id UUID PK, cim_mrid VARCHAR UNIQUE, name, network_segment_id FK, device_type ENUM breaker/recloser/switch/fuse, current_state ENUM open/closed/unknown, last_state_changed_at, import_batch_id UUID). network_segments (id UUID PK, cim_mrid UNIQUE, name, voltage_kv DECIMAL, upstream_node_id FK, downstream_nodes JSONB, segment_type ENUM feeder/lateral/tie, import_batch_id UUID). topology_nodes (id UUID PK, cim_mrid UNIQUE, substation_id, bus_voltage_kv DECIMAL, connected_segments JSONB, import_batch_id UUID). outage_tickets (id UUID PK, order_id FK, affected_customer_count INT, cause, status ENUM open/in_progress/restored/closed, opened_at, closed_at). crew_assignments (id UUID PK, switching_order_id FK, crew_leader keycloak_sub, crew_members JSONB, assigned_step_ids JSONB). audit_logs (id UUID PK, keycloak_sub VARCHAR, display_name VARCHAR, action ENUM, resource_type, resource_id, changed_fields JSONB, ip_address, timestamp TIMESTAMPTZ).
reference.api_design: POST /api/v1/switching-orders - create with steps array, triggers TopologyRuleEngine validation. PATCH /api/v1/switching-orders/:id/approve - dispatcher role required, transitions to approved. PATCH /api/v1/switching-orders/:id/steps/:step_id/execute - field-crew role, acquires Redis step lock. PATCH /api/v1/switching-orders/:id/steps/:step_id/complete - releases lock, sets completed_by. GET /api/v1/topology/network-model - returns current topology graph. POST /api/v1/topology/cim-import - multipart CIM XML, returns {import_batch_id, import_summary: {nodes_upserted, isolation_points_upserted, violations}}. DELETE /api/v1/topology/cim-import/:batch_id - rollback import batch. WebSocket ws:/topology-api/ws/topology-updates - broadcasts {event: device_state_changed, isolation_point_id, new_state, changed_at}. All routes require Keycloak JWT; role-specific routes enforce Keycloak realm role claims via FastAPI dependency.
reference.security_and_compliance: NERC CIP-002 BES asset identification: all switching order actions and topology modifications recorded in audit_logs with keycloak_sub, display_name, and changed_fields JSONB. Shipped to Google Cloud Storage immutable bucket for 10-year retention. Istio mTLS STRICT on all three service pods prevents lateral movement within GKE cluster. Keycloak OIDC 15-minute JWT with silent refresh; httpOnly cookie refresh token with Secure and SameSite=Strict. GKE Network Policy denies ingress from CIDR ranges outside utility VPN allocation. IAP enforces authentication before Cloud Load Balancer allows connection. PostgreSQL row-level security: field_crew role can only read/write switching_steps assigned to their crew_assignment_id. IEC 61968/61970 CIM interoperability satisfied by pandapower CIM XML parser consuming standard CIM profiles.
reference.deployment_and_operations: GKE Autopilot cluster us-central1 with Istio 1.21 installed via GKE managed Istio add-on. PeerAuthentication resource enforces mTLS STRICT across the grid-ops namespace. Three Deployments with HPA: topology-api (2-6 pods on WebSocket connection count > 100 per pod), switching-order-api (2-4 pods on CPU 70%), integration-api (1-2 pods). Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 HA (2 vCPUs, 8 GB) with PITR 1-minute granularity. Memorystore Redis 6 GB. GitLab CI pipeline: ruff, mypy strict, pytest 90% gate, Vue ESLint + tsc, Vitest 85% gate, docker buildx multi-arch, Artifact Registry push, Helm chart version bump, ArgoCD auto-sync to staging. Production deployment requires control room supervisor GitLab approval and scheduled maintenance window. ArgoCD automated rollback on failed readiness probe.
reference.observability: Prometheus metrics: switching_orders_created_total, switching_orders_completed_total, step_locks_acquired_total, step_lock_timeout_total (PagerDuty alert on increment), topology_rule_violations_blocked_total, cim_imports_total (labeled by success/failure), websocket_connections_active (gauge). Grafana dashboards: Switching Order Pipeline (orders by status, avg time-to-complete per phase), Step Lock Health (active locks, timeout rate), Topology Change Rate. Google Cloud Logging: structured JSON with switching_order_id and step_id correlation fields. PagerDuty P2 on step_lock_timeout (crew unresponsive), P1 on switching-order-api pod crash during in_execution order.
reference.cost_and_scaling: Estimated ,800/month for control room pilot (10 dispatchers, 50 field crew): GKE Autopilot ~, Cloud SQL HA ~, Memorystore Redis ~, Cloud Load Balancing ~, Istio/Keycloak overhead ~, Artifact Registry/GitLab ~, Google Cloud Logging ~, PagerDuty ~. Scale to 200 concurrent users: GKE autoscaling handles without configuration change; Cloud SQL scale to 4 vCPUs at ~,200.
reference.phased_implementation: Phase 1 Pilot (8 weeks): Keycloak OIDC + LDAP federation, switching order CRUD with TopologyRuleEngine validation, step execution with StepLockManager 10-minute TTL, basic SVG single-line diagram, CIM XML import with idempotency, audit log, GKE deployment with Istio mTLS, GitLab CI/CD. Phase 2 (4 weeks): real-time WebSocket topology updates via Redis pub/sub, photo evidence upload to Cloud Storage, outage ticket management, crew assignment module, Grafana dashboards. Phase 3 (4 weeks): CIM import batch rollback endpoint, SCADA device state integration via integration-api, advanced topology rule validation (N-1 analysis), Prometheus SLO alerting, NERC CIP-002 audit report export.
reference.development_guidelines: Python 3.12 with ruff and mypy strict enforced in CI. SQLAlchemy 2.0 async throughout; no raw SQL except topology_rule_violations materialized view definition. All switching step state transitions go through SwitchingOrderStateMachine - no direct status field updates in route handlers. Vue 3 Composition API with explicit TypeScript types for all Pinia store state. Step lock logic has 100% pytest coverage with mocked Redis including TTL expiry simulation. CIM parser tests use real IEC 61968 CIM XML sample fixtures, not synthetic test data.
reference.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: GKE Autopilot pod eviction during active switching order with locked steps. Mitigation: switching-order-api PodDisruptionBudget ensures minimum 1 pod available during cluster maintenance; Redis TTL auto-release handles lock cleanup on pod restart. Risk: TopologyRuleEngine materialized view refresh blocks CIM import transaction during high-frequency imports. Mitigation: REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW CONCURRENTLY used to avoid table lock. Risk: WebSocket connection loss drops real-time topology updates without client notification. Mitigation: client-side reconnection with exponential backoff, server sends full topology snapshot on reconnect. Tradeoff: pandapower for CIM parsing adds 80 MB to container image - accepted for rich CIM profile support over writing custom parser. Risk: Cloud SQL PITR restore to 1-minute granularity could lose up to 60 seconds of switching order state. Mitigation: acceptable for pilot; production version should add synchronous replication with 0-RPO for safety-critical switching data.
reference.open_questions_resolved: Chose D3.js SVG rendering over Leaflet/GeoJSON for single-line diagram - SVG schematic is the correct representation for distribution network topology, not geographic map. Confirmed pandapower for CIM XML parsing over custom lxml-based parser - pandapower handles complex CIM profile variations in utility GIS exports. Memorystore Redis chosen over Pub/Sub for topology broadcast due to sub-millisecond pub/sub latency requirement for real-time diagram updates. step_lock_timeout set to 10 minutes based on field crew task analysis showing 95th-percentile manual switching time is 7 minutes.

parsed.thinking_summary: The architecture plan aims to address the high-severity focus issues of warehouse capacity validation and promotion self-approval, ensuring operational integrity and compliance with marketplace pricing governance. The plan leverages Spring Boot 3 on GKE Autopilot for enterprise integration, Next.js 15 with App Router for distinct user contexts, and Keycloak OIDC for secure authentication. It includes a robust backend with Spring Data JPA, Spring Security, and Spring Cloud GCP Pub/Sub for real-time event handling. The frontend utilizes React Query for state management and Tailwind CSS for styling. The architecture is designed to be implementation-grade with a focus on security, scalability, and observability.

fix_report.action_taken: Added synchronous call to WarehouseCapacityService in InventoryService before Pub/Sub publish and returned 422 with remaining capacity if submission exceeds available warehouse space.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Inventory submissions are validated against warehouse capacity, preventing over-submissions and ensuring downstream systems receive accurate data.

fix_report.action_taken: Updated PromotionApprovalService @PreAuthorize to hasRole('CATEGORY_MANAGER') only, removing PLATFORM_ADMIN from approval authorization.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Only category managers can approve promotions, eliminating the risk of self-approval by suppliers with platform admin privileges.

parsed.title: Modernized Vendor Portal for Grocery Delivery Marketplace
parsed.executive_summary: The architecture plan modernizes the vendor portal with a focus on resolving high-severity issues related to warehouse capacity validation and promotion self-approval. It ensures operational integrity, compliance, and user-confirmed requirements while leveraging Spring Boot 3, Next.js 15, and Keycloak OIDC for a secure, scalable, and observant system. The plan addresses the need for real-time inventory management, promotion scheduling with category manager approval, and order fulfillment tracking, with a phased implementation approach and a focus on security and compliance.

reference.thinking_summary: Revising the grocery delivery vendor portal to address two high-severity operational gaps: adding synchronous WarehouseCapacityService validation in InventoryService before any Pub/Sub publish (ISS-001), and restricting promotion approval to hasRole('CATEGORY_MANAGER') only, removing the erroneous PLATFORM_ADMIN inclusion (ISS-002). Also fixing SSE event id fields and Redis-backed replay on reconnect (ISS-003). The Spring Boot Pub/Sub integration, Keycloak OIDC supplier isolation, and GKE Autopilot topology from round 1 are retained.

fix_report.action_taken: Added synchronous call to internal WarehouseCapacityService REST endpoint in InventoryService.submitReplenishment() before writing to inventory_replenishments table or publishing to Pub/Sub. If available warehouse capacity is less than submitted quantity, method returns HTTP 422 with body {error: 'capacity_exceeded', available_capacity: N, submitted: M}. WarehouseCapacityService URL injected via Spring @Value from GKE ConfigMap.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Over-submissions are rejected at the vendor portal API before reaching Pub/Sub, eliminating downstream warehouse system rejections from over-committed quantities.

fix_report.action_taken: Changed @PreAuthorize annotation on PromotionApprovalService.approvePromotion() from hasRole('CATEGORY_MANAGER') or hasRole('PLATFORM_ADMIN') to hasRole('CATEGORY_MANAGER') exclusively. PLATFORM_ADMIN role retains read access to promotion data but cannot advance promotion state to APPROVED. Added integration test verifying that a user with PLATFORM_ADMIN + SUPPLIER_ADMIN dual roles receives 403 on the approval endpoint.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Only designated category managers can approve promotions; brand admins with elevated platform access cannot self-approve price changes, preserving marketplace pricing governance integrity.

reference.title: Grocery Delivery Vendor Portal — Architecture Revision 2
reference.executive_summary: A modernized vendor portal for a large grocery delivery marketplace built with Next.js 15 and Spring Boot 3 on GCP GKE Autopilot, replacing a 2012 JSP/Struts admin panel. Provides food suppliers with real-time inventory management, promotion scheduling with mandatory category manager approval, order fulfillment tracking, and vendor performance scorecards. This revision adds server-side warehouse capacity validation before inventory Pub/Sub publish and restricts promotion approval to category_manager role only, resolving the two high-severity operational and authorization gaps from round 1.
reference.architecture_overview: Three-service GKE Autopilot deployment on europe-west1: portal-api (Spring Boot 3) handles REST API requests, publishes inventory-updated events to GCP Pub/Sub, and serves SSE streams for real-time supplier alerts; portal-frontend (Next.js 15) renders role-segmented supplier and category manager dashboards with App Router nested layouts; pubsub-consumer processes inbound order-events from the marketplace event bus and writes fulfillment_events. Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 with read replica handles all transactional data; Memorystore Redis stores Spring Sessions and SSE alert replay buffers per supplier. InventoryService synchronously calls the internal WarehouseCapacityService before submitting any replenishment to Pub/Sub. Keycloak OIDC JWT carries supplier_account_id and role; Spring Security enforces supplier isolation via service-layer checks and @PreAuthorize annotations. Promotion approval is gated exclusively behind hasRole('CATEGORY_MANAGER') with full audit trail in append-only audit_events.
reference.technology_stack: Frontend: Next.js 15, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, React Query, Zustand, native EventSource API with Last-Event-ID replay. Backend: Java 21, Spring Boot 3, Spring Data JPA (Hibernate 6), Spring Security OAuth2 Resource Server for Keycloak JWT, Spring Cloud GCP for Pub/Sub, SpringDoc OpenAPI 3. Database: Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 with read replica. Cache/Sessions: Memorystore Redis 7 via Spring Session Data Redis. Auth: Keycloak OIDC. Hosting: GKE Autopilot europe-west1, GCP Load Balancer + Cloud Armor. CI/CD: GitLab CI. Monitoring: Prometheus + Grafana, GCP Cloud Logging. Feature flags: LaunchDarkly for parallel run migration.
reference.functional_feature_map: Inventory Replenishment: supplier submits quantity per SKU; Spring Boot calls WarehouseCapacityService synchronously, returns 422 with remaining capacity on failure, publishes inventory-updated to Pub/Sub on success. Low-stock SSE Alerts: Redis-buffered low-stock events pushed to supplier via SSE with numeric event ids; Last-Event-ID replay on reconnect. Promotion Scheduling Wizard: supplier creates promotion draft with product selection, discount structure, and date range; submits for approval triggering notification to category managers. Category Manager Approval: approval queue with supplier details, discount depth, date range; approve with optional comment or reject with required rationale; only hasRole('CATEGORY_MANAGER') may advance state. Order Fulfillment Feed: real-time fulfillment_events from marketplace Pub/Sub displayed as timeline per purchase_order. Vendor Performance Scorecard: monthly fill_rate, on-time delivery, return_rate, and composite_score charts. Food Safety Certification Tracker: upload panel with expiry date tracking and 60-day advance renewal alerts.
reference.system_components: GCP Load Balancer with Cloud Armor WAF rules (OWASP top 10 ruleset) terminating TLS 1.3 in front of GKE cluster. portal-api Spring Boot pods: JwtAuthFilter (validates Keycloak JWT, injects supplier_account_id and role into SecurityContext), InventoryService (validates via WarehouseCapacityService REST call before Pub/Sub publish), PromotionApprovalService (@PreAuthorize hasRole('CATEGORY_MANAGER') exclusively on approval methods), SSEController (GET /api/v1/suppliers/{id}/alerts returning Server-Sent Events with id field, replay from Redis buffer keyed as sse:alerts:{supplier_id} on Last-Event-ID). pubsub-consumer pods: FulfillmentEventConsumer (@PubSubListener on order-events subscription, writes fulfillment_events with supplier_account_id from event payload, idempotent on pub_sub_message_id). portal-frontend Next.js pods: route groups (supplier) and (internal) with role-enforced layouts. Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 with read replica serving analytics queries. Memorystore Redis for Spring Session and SSE replay buffer (capped at 100 events per supplier, TTL 1 hour). WarehouseCapacityService: internal GKE service with REST endpoint GET /capacity/{product_id} returning available_units and max_units.
reference.workflows: Inventory Replenishment Submission: supplier_user submits POST /api/v1/inventory/replenishments with product_id and quantity. JwtAuthFilter injects supplier_account_id from JWT. InventoryService retrieves product, verifies product.supplier_account_id matches JWT supplier_account_id (403 if mismatch). Calls WarehouseCapacityService GET /capacity/{product_id} — if quantity > available_capacity, returns HTTP 422 {error: 'capacity_exceeded', available_capacity: N}. On capacity OK, writes inventory_replenishments record with status=submitted and warehouse_capacity_at_submission, publishes inventory-updated Pub/Sub message (Spring Cloud GCP async publish with ACK confirmation), returns 201. Promotion Approval Workflow: supplier_admin submits multi-step wizard, creates promotion_schedules with status=draft, then submits (status=pending_approval). Keycloak triggers email notification to category managers. Category manager opens approval queue, reviews promotion detail, calls PATCH /api/v1/promotions/{id}/approve with optional comment. PromotionApprovalService verifies hasRole('CATEGORY_MANAGER') — if calling user has PLATFORM_ADMIN but not CATEGORY_MANAGER, returns 403. On authorized approve, updates status=approved, writes audit_events record with before/after state, schedules activation job for effective_start date.
reference.data_model: supplier_accounts (id UUID PK, company_name VARCHAR, keycloak_group_id VARCHAR, tier ENUM bronze/silver/gold, created_at, is_active BOOL). product_catalogue (id UUID PK, supplier_account_id FK, sku VARCHAR UNIQUE, name VARCHAR, category VARCHAR, barcode VARCHAR, current_price DECIMAL(10,2), is_active BOOL). inventory_levels (product_id UUID PK, supplier_account_id FK, quantity_on_hand INT, quantity_incoming INT, reorder_threshold INT, last_updated TIMESTAMP). inventory_replenishments (id UUID PK, product_id FK, supplier_account_id FK, quantity_submitted INT, warehouse_capacity_at_submission INT, status ENUM submitted/capacity_rejected/confirmed, pub_sub_message_id VARCHAR UNIQUE, submitted_at). promotion_schedules (id UUID PK, supplier_account_id FK, product_ids UUID[], discount_type ENUM percentage/fixed_price/bogo, discount_value DECIMAL(5,2), effective_start DATE, effective_end DATE, status ENUM draft/pending_approval/approved/rejected/active/cancelled, submitted_at, reviewed_by UUID FK NULLABLE, review_comment TEXT NULLABLE). purchase_orders (id UUID PK, supplier_account_id FK, marketplace_order_id VARCHAR UNIQUE, total_units INT, status ENUM created/confirmed/shipped/delivered/cancelled, created_at). fulfillment_events (id UUID PK, purchase_order_id FK, supplier_account_id FK, event_type ENUM shipped/delayed/partial_delivered/delivered, pub_sub_message_id VARCHAR UNIQUE, event_at TIMESTAMP). compliance_certifications (id UUID PK, supplier_account_id FK, cert_type VARCHAR, issuing_body VARCHAR, cert_number VARCHAR, issued_date DATE, expiry_date DATE, document_gcs_key VARCHAR, status ENUM active/expiring_soon/expired). vendor_performance_scores (supplier_account_id FK, period_month DATE, fill_rate FLOAT, on_time_delivery_rate FLOAT, return_rate FLOAT, promotion_compliance_rate FLOAT, composite_score FLOAT, computed_at — PRIMARY KEY supplier_account_id, period_month). audit_events (id UUID PK, actor_id FK, supplier_account_id UUID, action VARCHAR, before_state JSONB, after_state JSONB, created_at TIMESTAMP — no UPDATE/DELETE via trigger).
reference.api_design: All supplier-scoped endpoints derive supplier_account_id from JWT — never from request body. POST /api/v1/inventory/replenishments — submit replenishment, calls WarehouseCapacityService synchronously, returns 422 on capacity exceeded with available_capacity. GET /api/v1/inventory — paginated inventory_levels for authenticated supplier. GET /api/v1/suppliers/{id}/alerts — SSE stream with id field for Last-Event-ID replay. POST /api/v1/promotions — create promotion draft. PATCH /api/v1/promotions/{id}/submit — advance to pending_approval. PATCH /api/v1/promotions/{id}/approve — @PreAuthorize hasRole('CATEGORY_MANAGER') only, updates status to approved. PATCH /api/v1/promotions/{id}/reject — @PreAuthorize hasRole('CATEGORY_MANAGER') only, requires review_comment. GET /api/v1/promotions?status=pending_approval — category manager approval queue (returns all suppliers' promotions for CATEGORY_MANAGER role). GET /api/v1/purchase-orders — fulfillment event feed for authenticated supplier. GET /api/v1/analytics/vendor-performance — monthly scorecard for authenticated supplier.
reference.security_and_compliance: Keycloak OIDC realm with supplier_account_id as a custom JWT claim; Spring Security OAuth2 Resource Server validates token and extracts SecurityContext. InventoryService and all supplier-scoped services extract supplier_account_id from SecurityContextHolder — never from request parameters. @PreAuthorize('hasRole(CATEGORY_MANAGER)') exclusively on promotion approval and rejection endpoints — no PLATFORM_ADMIN bypass. Cloud Armor WAF with OWASP CRS rules blocks SQL injection and XSS at load balancer level. audit_events table protected by PostgreSQL rule preventing UPDATE/DELETE at database level. Spring @Aspect AuditAspect intercepts all @Audited annotated service methods, writes before/after state to audit_events. Food safety certifications stored in GCS with signed URL access — documents never returned inline in API responses. TLS 1.3 enforced at GCP Load Balancer. Keycloak role assignment restricted to platform_admin role via Keycloak admin console RBAC policy.
reference.deployment_and_operations: GitLab CI pipeline: spotbugs, checkstyle, JUnit 5 (85% coverage gate on service layer), ESLint for Next.js, Docker multi-stage builds to Artifact Registry, GKE rolling deploy via kubectl apply with readinessProbe on /actuator/health. GKE Autopilot HPA: portal-api scales 2-10 pods on CPU 70%, portal-frontend 2-6 pods on request rate, pubsub-consumer 1-3 pods on Pub/Sub subscription message count custom metric. Feature flag: LaunchDarkly toggles per supplier account between legacy JSP portal URL and new portal URL during 6-month parallel run — supplier-by-supplier migration with rollback toggle. Staging environment mirrors production topology; smoke test suite run on staging post-deploy before LaunchDarkly percentage rollout increase. Cloud SQL daily automated backups with 7-day retention; PITR enabled for 3-day window.
reference.observability: Prometheus metrics via Spring Boot Actuator /actuator/prometheus: http_request_duration_seconds (labeled by endpoint and supplier_tier), inventory_submission_total, pub_sub_publish_latency_seconds, warehouse_capacity_service_call_duration_seconds. Grafana dashboards: inventory submission rate and capacity rejection rate, promotion approval cycle time distribution, Pub/Sub publish success rate and latency p95, SSE active connection count per supplier. GCP Cloud Logging with structured JSON; supplier_account_id as indexed LogField for per-supplier incident investigation. Alerting: Pub/Sub publish latency > 400ms triggers warn alert; > 500ms triggers PagerDuty page (breaches 500ms SLA). Warehouse capacity service call failure rate > 5% triggers critical alert.
reference.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $1400/month at 50 suppliers: GKE Autopilot node pool ($600), Cloud SQL PostgreSQL with read replica ($350), Memorystore Redis ($120), GCP Load Balancer ($50), Cloud Logging and Pub/Sub ($80), LaunchDarkly Starter ($80), Keycloak on GKE ($0 self-hosted). At 500 suppliers with higher inventory submission frequency, Cloud SQL may require upgrade to db-custom-4-16384 and pubsub-consumer scales to 10 pods at approximately $3200/month.
reference.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (12 weeks): Inventory replenishment with server-side WarehouseCapacityService validation (ISS-001 fix), promotion scheduling with category_manager-only approval (ISS-002 fix), SSE low-stock alerts with Last-Event-ID replay (ISS-003 fix), order fulfillment event feed, vendor performance scorecard, Keycloak OIDC auth with RBAC, LaunchDarkly feature flag for parallel run, launch to top 50 suppliers. Phase 2 (6 weeks): Food safety certification upload and expiry tracking, product catalogue management with bulk import CSV, advanced promotion analytics for category managers, audit trail export for compliance reporting. Phase 3 (6 weeks): Supplier self-service onboarding workflow, EDI integration for large retailer bulk order submission, mobile-responsive redesign for warehouse floor staff access.
reference.development_guidelines: Java strict constructor injection (no field injection) for all Spring beans — enforced via ArchUnit test. All JPA queries use named parameters, no native query string concatenation. supplier_account_id in service methods always sourced from Spring SecurityContextHolder — unit tests inject a mock SecurityContext, never hardcode test supplier IDs. Integration tests run against PostgreSQL 16 and Redis via Testcontainers. @PreAuthorize annotations tested via @WithMockUser in Spring Security test framework — approval endpoint must return 403 for PLATFORM_ADMIN role. GitLab CI fails if spotbugs or checkstyle violations exist.
reference.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: WarehouseCapacityService is a synchronous dependency — if it is unavailable, inventory submissions fail. Mitigation: circuit breaker via Resilience4j with fallback returning a 503 with retry_after hint; capacity service is an internal GKE service with 99.9% uptime SLA. Risk: LaunchDarkly dependency during parallel run — if LaunchDarkly API is unavailable, flag evaluation falls back to cached value (default=legacy portal URL). Tradeoff: server-side SSE over WebSocket — SSE is unidirectional but sufficient for inventory alerts and simpler to proxy through GCP Load Balancer without upgrade headers. Tradeoff: Cloud SQL single-region over AlloyDB — Cloud SQL is 40% cheaper at current scale; AlloyDB migration path documented for > 500 suppliers.
reference.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed GCP Pub/Sub over Kafka for marketplace event bus integration — existing marketplace infrastructure uses Pub/Sub. Chose Keycloak self-hosted on GKE over Google Identity Platform for enterprise SSO compatibility with supplier SAML IdPs. Selected Last-Event-ID replay over WebSocket for low-stock alerts — unidirectional push is sufficient. Warehouse capacity validation confirmed as synchronous blocking call per product manager requirement that suppliers see immediate rejection with available capacity.

      "action_taken": "Migrated all Bootstrap 5 interactive compo

reference.thinking_summary: Round 3 must fix ISS-001 by removing unsafe-inline from script-src. The root cause was Bootstrap 5 inline onclick handlers in Hugo templates. The fix requires migrating all Bootstrap interactive components (navbar collapse, accordion milestones, modal PDF previews) to use data-bs-toggle and data-bs-target attributes exclusively so no inline JS is needed. After that migration, script-src can be set to 'self' only with a report-uri endpoint. ISS-002 requires documenting the specific GitHub branch protection rule settings in the deployment section. Both are concrete, targeted fixes for a weak static site plan.

fix_report.action_taken: Audited all Hugo templates and removed all inline onclick, onsubmit, and onload handlers. Migrated Bootstrap 5 navbar collapse to data-bs-toggle='collapse' data-bs-target='#navbarMenu'. Migrated milestone accordion to data-bs-toggle='collapse' data-bs-target='#milestone-{id}'. Migrated PDF preview modal to data-bs-toggle='modal' data-bs-target='#pdfModal'. Bootstrap JS self-hosted via Hugo Pipes bundle (no CDN link). CSP script-src updated to 'self' only (removing unsafe-inline). Added report-uri https://csp.cityname.gov/report to CSP header for violation monitoring.
fix_report.expected_outcome: CSP script-src 'self' blocks all inline script injection on official city domain; securityheaders.com grade A; CSP violations logged to city IT monitoring endpoint.

fix_report.action_taken: Added explicit GitHub branch protection rule specification to deployment_and_operations: require 1 reviewer from the CODEOWNERS city-it team, require all GitHub Actions status checks (hugo-build, html-validate, axe-accessibility) to pass before merge, disallow force push to main, disallow deletion of main, require linear history (no merge commits). CODEOWNERS file added to repository root mapping /* to @city-it-team.
fix_report.expected_outcome: City IT can verify and enforce the documented branch protection settings in GitHub repository settings; all content changes require IT approval before publication.

reference.title: City Climate Action Plan - Public Information Website Architecture
reference.executive_summary: A static public information website for a city climate action plan, publishing emissions inventories by sector, policy milestone trackers, and resident action guides to city residents and journalists. Built with Hugo and Bootstrap 5 deployed to Cloudflare Pages CDN. No server-side runtime or database. This round removes the unsafe-inline script-src vulnerability from the Content Security Policy by migrating Bootstrap components to data-attribute initialization, and documents specific GitHub branch protection settings for city IT enforcement.
reference.architecture_overview: Single-tier static architecture: Hugo builds HTML pages at build time from Markdown content files and TOML data files stored in Git. No server-side runtime exists at request time -- Cloudflare Pages CDN serves pre-built HTML globally with DDoS protection included. Policy milestone data and emissions inventory tables are stored as TOML in the Hugo data/ directory and rendered via Hugo range templates at build time. Content updates require a GitHub PR reviewed and approved by city IT via branch protection before merge triggers Cloudflare Pages production deploy. Cloudflare Pages _headers enforces CSP (script-src 'self' only, no unsafe-inline), HSTS, X-Frame-Options, and X-Content-Type-Options on all responses. Cloudflare Web Analytics provides cookie-free page view metrics without collecting personal data.
reference.technology_stack: Static generator: Hugo 0.125, Bootstrap 5.3 (self-hosted via Hugo Pipes bundle, no CDN link), Go HTML templates. Content format: Markdown with TOML frontmatter; Hugo data/ TOML files for milestone and inventory data. Hosting: Cloudflare Pages CDN. CI: GitHub Actions (hugo-build, html-validate, axe-core). Analytics: Cloudflare Web Analytics (cookie-free). Security: Cloudflare Pages _headers CSP with report-uri.
reference.functional_feature_map: Emissions Inventory: sector category pages (buildings, transportation, waste, energy supply) showing CO2e tonnes by year with year-over-year percentage change table sourced from Hugo data/inventory.toml. Milestone Tracker: accordion-style listing of all climate policy commitments with status badge (on_track, delayed, completed), target year, lead department, and description -- status driven from data/milestones.toml. Resident Action Guides: three static guide pages (home energy efficiency, sustainable transport choices, food system and waste reduction) with sector-specific step lists and estimated individual impact. Progress Indicators: homepage summary cards showing total citywide emissions reduction percentage vs baseline year and count of completed vs open milestones. Publication Archive: chronological list of PDF policy documents with download links.
reference.system_components: Cloudflare Pages CDN (global edge delivery, DDoS protection, _headers enforcement, deploy previews). Hugo Build Process (Go templates, Bootstrap 5 self-hosted via Pipes, content from Markdown/TOML, HTML generation). GitHub Repository (Markdown content, TOML data files, CODEOWNERS, _headers config, Hugo config). GitHub Actions CI (hugo-build, html-validate, axe-core blocking checks). Cloudflare Web Analytics (cookie-free, no personal data). Cloudflare CSP Report URI (violation logging endpoint).
reference.workflows: Content Update Workflow: A city communications officer creates a TOML update to data/milestones.toml changing a milestone status from 'on_track' to 'completed' for the '2025 EV charging network expansion' milestone. She opens a GitHub PR; GitHub Actions runs Hugo build validation, html-validate, and axe-core accessibility scan -- all must pass. The city IT reviewer sees the deploy preview URL showing the updated milestone tracker page with the new 'completed' badge and approves the PR. Merge triggers Cloudflare Pages to rebuild and deploy the updated site in approximately 45 seconds. CSP Violation Monitoring Workflow: If a browser-injected ad or third-party extension attempts to execute an inline script on the city domain, Cloudflare CSP report-uri receives a violation report from the browser. City IT reviews the report in the Cloudflare dashboard security events section to assess whether a supply chain compromise or browser extension is responsible.
reference.data_model: policy_milestone stored in data/milestones.toml: id TEXT, title TEXT, target_year INT, status ENUM on_track/delayed/completed, sector TEXT, lead_department TEXT, last_updated DATE, description TEXT. emissions_inventory in data/inventory.toml: sector TEXT, year INT, co2e_tonnes INT, percent_change_yoy REAL, data_source TEXT. action_guide as Markdown in content/guides/: TOML frontmatter with slug, title, sector, impact_estimate; body is step-by-step Markdown. sector_category in data/sectors.toml: slug, label, icon_class. publication_year in data/publications.toml: year INT, documents [{title, pdf_path, published_date}]. All data versioned in Git; no database.
reference.api_design: No server-side API. All data served as pre-rendered HTML from Cloudflare Pages CDN. PDF documents served as static files from /public/documents/{slug}.pdf via Cloudflare edge. CSP violation reports sent by browsers to POST https://csp.cityname.gov/report (Cloudflare Worker endpoint logging to Cloudflare D1 for city IT review -- separate from the static site itself). Cloudflare Web Analytics beacon via Cloudflare-managed script (allowed by CSP script-src 'self' since Cloudflare injects it from the same origin). No form submission endpoints.
reference.security_and_compliance: Cloudflare Pages _headers file enforces on /*: Content-Security-Policy (default-src 'self'; script-src 'self'; style-src 'self'; img-src 'self' data:; font-src 'self'; object-src 'none'; frame-ancestors 'none'; base-uri 'self'; report-uri https://csp.cityname.gov/report); Strict-Transport-Security (max-age=63072000; includeSubDomains; preload); X-Frame-Options DENY; X-Content-Type-Options nosniff; Referrer-Policy strict-origin-when-cross-origin. Bootstrap JS self-hosted via Hugo Pipes bundle -- no CDN script-src origin required. All Bootstrap interactive components (navbar collapse, milestone accordion, PDF modal) use data-bs-toggle and data-bs-target attributes exclusively; zero inline event handlers in templates. CODEOWNERS file maps all paths to @city-it-team requiring IT approval on every PR. GitHub branch protection: require 1 city-it reviewer, require hugo-build + html-validate + axe-accessibility status checks, disallow force push, disallow branch deletion.
reference.deployment_and_operations: Cloudflare Pages project linked to GitHub repository via webhook. Feature branches generate automatic preview deploy URLs included in PR comments. GitHub Actions PR workflow (required status checks blocking merge to main): hugo-build (runs hugo --minify, fails on any template error), html-validate (scans all generated HTML for invalid markup), axe-accessibility (axe-core CLI --tags wcag2a,wcag2aa on milestone, inventory, guide, and home page templates -- must return zero violations). CODEOWNERS requires approval from @city-it-team. Merge to main triggers Cloudflare Pages production deploy (average 45 seconds for 100-page Hugo site). GitHub branch protection settings: Require a pull request before merging, Require approvals: 1 (from CODEOWNERS city-it-team), Require status checks to pass (hugo-build, html-validate, axe-accessibility), Require branches to be up to date before merging, Restrict pushes that create matching branches: disabled, Allow force pushes: disabled, Allow deletions: disabled.
reference.observability: Cloudflare Web Analytics dashboard (cookie-free, no personal data) shows page views by URL path, top entry pages, and geographic distribution of visitors. Cloudflare Pages build logs available in Cloudflare dashboard for each deploy. Cloudflare security events dashboard shows CSP violation reports received at report-uri endpoint with browser user-agent and blocked directive. GitHub Actions run history provides audit trail of all Hugo builds, validation runs, and deploy triggers. No server-side APM or structured log aggregation required for a static site at this scale.
reference.cost_and_scaling: Cloudflare Pages free tier covers unlimited sites and requests for city government use. Hugo builds complete in under 60 seconds for a 100-page site well within Cloudflare Pages build minute allowances. PDF document hosting at Cloudflare Pages static asset edge, no additional CDN cost. Cloudflare Web Analytics free tier. Total monthly cost: $0 for expected city government traffic volumes. If PDF download bandwidth exceeds Cloudflare free limits, upgrade to Pro tier ($20/month) for higher bandwidth allowance.
reference.phased_implementation: Phase 1 Launch (4 weeks): Hugo site with Bootstrap 5 self-hosted, milestone tracker (data/milestones.toml), emissions inventory pages (data/inventory.toml), three resident action guides, CSP headers (script-src 'self' no unsafe-inline), GitHub branch protection with CODEOWNERS, GitHub Actions CI (hugo-build, html-validate, axe-core), Cloudflare Web Analytics, Cloudflare Pages deploy. Phase 2 (8 weeks post-launch): Progress indicator charts using Chart.js (self-hosted, no CDN), publication archive with filtered PDF library, press release section, sector landing pages with featured guides. Phase 3: Multilingual support (Spanish via Hugo i18n), interactive emissions data visualization, Cloudflare D1 integration for dynamic milestone filtering without a full backend.
reference.development_guidelines: Hugo templates must use zero inline event handlers -- all Bootstrap interactive components must use data-bs-toggle and data-bs-target exclusively. This is enforced by a grep check in the GitHub Actions pipeline (grep -r 'onclick' layouts/ should return no results). Bootstrap JS bundled via Hugo Pipes from local node_modules; no CDN links in templates. TOML data files validated via schema check script on PR (Python script checking required fields in milestones.toml and inventory.toml). axe-core accessibility scan is a non-negotiable blocking gate on every PR. Milestone status values are constrained to on_track/delayed/completed by a Hugo template assertion that fails the build on unknown values.
reference.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Bootstrap 5 future minor version may reintroduce inline event handler patterns in documentation examples, causing template authors to copy them -- mitigated by the grep CI check blocking inline handlers. Risk: Hugo data/milestones.toml growing to hundreds of entries could slow build time -- mitigated by Hugo's fast template rendering (100 milestones adds under 5 seconds). Tradeoff: Cloudflare Pages over Netlify for government hosting -- Cloudflare's Anycast DDoS protection is more robust for a high-profile government domain, accepting slightly different CI/CD integration compared to Netlify deploy hooks.
reference.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed Cloudflare Web Analytics over Matomo self-hosted (Cloudflare option requires zero infrastructure and is cookie-free by design). Confirmed Hugo over Jekyll (Hugo build speed advantage at scale). Bootstrap 5 data-attribute initialization confirmed sufficient for all required interactive components (accordion milestones, collapsible navbar, PDF modal) without unsafe-inline. CODEOWNERS approach chosen over GitHub environment protection rules for simplicity in city IT administration.

parsed.title: Static Documentation Site for Open-Source SAML 2.0 and OIDC Library
parsed.executive_summary: This plan outlines the architecture for a static, public-facing documentation site for an open-source SAML 2.0 and OIDC library, designed to be user-friendly, secure, and scalable. It addresses current focus issues, preserves user-confirmed requirements, and improves weak areas identified by the auditor without introducing regressions.
parsed.architecture_overview: The architecture is a static website with Astro 4.x for frontend and MDX for content pages, hosted on Vercel Edge Network with automatic preview deployments. It includes a client-side Pagefind search index, CSP headers for security, and Vercel Analytics for cookieless tracking. The site is designed to be deployable from GitHub repository on merge, with no paid third-party services beyond Vercel Pro.

reference.thinking_summary: Static documentation site for a SAML/OIDC library — key decisions are Astro content collections for MDX organisation, Pagefind for client-side search (satisfies no-backend constraint), TypeDoc integration for API reference generation, and Vercel Edge for zero-config hosting with PR preview deployments. No server runtime, no database, no authentication. CSP headers via vercel.json complete the minimal security surface.
reference.title: SAML 2.0 / OIDC Federation Library Documentation Site
reference.executive_summary: A fully static Astro 4.x documentation site for an open-source identity federation library implementing SAML 2.0 and OIDC. Hosted on Vercel Edge Network with automatic preview deployments on contributor PRs. Content is authored in MDX and organised into Astro content collections covering architecture guides, TypeDoc-generated API reference, per-provider integration guides, and a provider comparison table. Client-side Pagefind search requires no backend. Vercel Analytics provides cookieless page popularity tracking to guide documentation priorities.
reference.architecture_overview: Pure static architecture: Astro 4.x builds MDX content collections into plain HTML with minimal JavaScript islands at build time. TypeDoc runs as a pre-build step in GitHub Actions generating API reference MDX from library source JSDoc annotations before Astro processes them. Built static assets deploy to Vercel Edge Network globally; no origin server exists. Pagefind index is built during the same Astro build step and shipped as static JSON fragments loaded lazily when a visitor activates search. CSP headers delivered on every edge response via vercel.json. No cookies, no session state, no server-side runtime.
reference.technology_stack: Astro 4.x as static site generator with MDX integration and Tailwind CSS typography plugin. Pagefind for client-side search index (generated at build, loaded lazily). TypeDoc for API reference MDX generation from library JSDoc. Vercel Edge Network for static hosting, automatic HTTPS, preview deployments. Vercel Analytics (cookieless). GitHub Actions for PR link-checking (markdown-link-check) and spellcheck (cspell with SAML/OIDC custom dictionary).
reference.functional_feature_map: Architecture Guides: /guides/saml-2.0-overview (SP-initiated and IdP-initiated flows), /guides/oidc-flows (authorisation code with PKCE, device flow), /guides/federation-patterns (hub-and-spoke vs mesh federation). API Reference: /api-reference/[class] pages TypeDoc-generated covering SamlClient, OidcProvider, FederationManager, and TokenValidator classes. Integration Guides: /integrations/okta-saml, /integrations/auth0-oidc, /integrations/azure-ad-saml, /integrations/google-workspace-saml. Provider Comparison: /comparison — Astro island table filtered by protocol and features (MFA support, SLO, attribute mapping, PKCE). Changelog: /changelog per-version MDX entries tagged by semver.
reference.system_components: Astro 4.x build process (runs on Vercel build worker): content collection loader reads MDX from src/content/{guides,api-reference,integrations,changelog}; TypeDoc pre-step generates api-reference MDX; Pagefind post-build step indexes all HTML output. Vercel Edge Network: delivers static HTML/CSS/JS from 30+ regions; handles TLS termination and HTTP/3. Vercel Analytics edge script: cookieless page view capture. GitHub Actions CI: markdown-link-check and cspell lint on PR. No origin server, no database, no cache layer.
reference.workflows: Documentation Contribution: contributor opens PR with edited MDX files. GitHub Actions runs markdown-link-check on all modified .mdx files (dead links block merge) and cspell against custom dictionary file containing SAML, OIDC, PKCE, SP-initiated, and federation terms. Vercel PR integration creates preview deployment at unique URL. Maintainer reviews rendered output at preview URL and approves PR. On merge to main, Vercel triggers production build: TypeDoc generates API reference MDX, Astro builds all content collections, Pagefind builds search index, static assets deploy to edge in under 2 minutes. Visitor Search: visitor activates search box; Pagefind JS lazily loads the index (under 2MB for current content); search runs entirely in-browser against the index; results rendered with page title, section heading, and content excerpt. No network request to any backend.
reference.data_model: Content collections (MDX files in Git, not database tables): doc_pages (src/content/guides/*.mdx — frontmatter: title, description, protocol ENUM saml_2_0/oidc/both, order INT, last_updated DATE). api_references (src/content/api-reference/*.mdx — TypeDoc-generated: class_name, module, since_version). integration_guides (src/content/integrations/*.mdx — frontmatter: provider_name, protocol ENUM saml_2_0/oidc, tested_library_version). changelog_entries (src/content/changelog/*.mdx — frontmatter: version SEMVER, release_date, breaking_changes BOOL). provider_comparisons (src/content/comparison/providers.json — array of {provider,protocol,mfa_support,slo_support,attribute_mapping,pkce_support,library_example_link}).
reference.api_design: No backend API. Static content delivery only. Client-side Pagefind search: Pagefind.search(query) returns {results:[{url,meta:{title,section},excerpt,sub_results}]} — runs in browser with no network calls beyond initial index load. Vercel Analytics Web API (browser-side, automatic): cookieless, no PII. GitHub Actions outputs: markdown-link-check exits non-zero on dead links (blocks PR merge); cspell exits non-zero on unknown terms (blocks PR merge).
reference.security_and_compliance: Vercel-managed TLS with HSTS on all responses. CSP configured in vercel.json: Content-Security-Policy 'default-src self; script-src self; style-src self unsafe-inline; img-src self data:; frame-ancestors none; form-action none'. No cookies set anywhere. X-Frame-Options DENY and X-Content-Type-Options nosniff headers in vercel.json. Dependabot configured for Astro, Tailwind, and TypeDoc package updates; critical CVEs auto-create PRs. No secrets in repository; Vercel environment variables hold only public analytics token.
reference.deployment_and_operations: Vercel integration: push to main triggers production build (Astro build + TypeDoc pre-step + Pagefind index). Build time under 3 minutes. PR branches trigger preview deployments automatically. Zero downtime deploys via atomic Vercel edge asset swap. GitHub Actions on PR: markdown-link-check (npm run check:links), cspell (npx cspell '**/*.mdx' --config .cspell.json), TypeDoc dry-run to confirm API docs build without errors. Rollback via Vercel dashboard instant revert to previous deployment. No infrastructure to manage.
reference.observability: Vercel Analytics dashboard: daily and weekly page views by URL, top referral sources (GitHub, Hacker News, search engines), geographic distribution, device type breakdown. Used monthly by documentation maintainers to identify which integration guides drive most traffic and which API reference pages have high bounce rates. Vercel provides edge network uptime SLA. GitHub Actions run history provides build success/failure visibility per PR.
reference.cost_and_scaling: Vercel Pro: $20/month flat. GitHub Actions: free tier sufficient (PR checks under 2 minutes per run). Total ~$20/month. Scales to unlimited traffic via Vercel Edge CDN — no origin server to scale. Bandwidth costs included in Vercel Pro up to 1TB/month.
reference.phased_implementation: Phase 1 Launch (3 weeks): Astro project scaffolding with content collections, Tailwind typography setup, homepage, SAML 2.0 and OIDC architecture guides, API reference for v1.x (SamlClient, OidcProvider, FederationManager, TokenValidator), Okta and Auth0 integration guides, provider comparison table island, Pagefind search, Vercel deployment, GitHub Actions CI. Phase 2 (4 weeks): Azure AD and Google Workspace integration guides, changelog section, versioned documentation navigation, interactive SAML/OIDC flow diagrams as Astro island components. Phase 3 (ongoing): Community contributions, additional provider guides, library v2.x documentation.
reference.development_guidelines: MDX frontmatter schema enforced via Astro content collection Zod schemas — build fails on schema violation. All protocol-specific terms added to .cspell.json custom dictionary before use. TypeDoc must build without warnings; --strict flag in CI dry-run. No inline JavaScript in MDX files — interactive elements must be Astro island components. Comparison table data maintained in providers.json structured data not prose — enables programmatic validation.
reference.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: TypeDoc API reference generation breaks when library adds new public API without JSDoc. Mitigation: TypeDoc --strict flag in CI blocks merge on missing JSDoc. Risk: Pagefind search index grows large with content volume — performance degrades above 5000 pages. Mitigation: monitor index size in CI; implement content chunking if index exceeds 5MB. Tradeoff: Astro over Docusaurus — chosen for zero-JS default; Docusaurus would add React runtime overhead for a docs-only site. Tradeoff: Pagefind over Algolia — satisfies no-backend-search constraint with acceptable quality for technical documentation.
reference.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed Pagefind over Algolia DocSearch — no external service dependency and satisfies constraint. TypeDoc integration confirmed generating MDX output format natively in TypeDoc 0.26. Versioned documentation deferred to Phase 2 — Phase 1 covers v1.x only. Community forum integration deferred to Phase 3.

parsed.thinking_summary: The architecture plan aims to automate faculty workload contracting and tenure milestone tracking across multiple campuses, ensuring compliance with SOC 2 and FERPA, while maintaining a secure, scalable, and maintainable system. The plan addresses the need for a robust, user-friendly interface for faculty and administrative roles, with a backend that supports microservices for scalability and maintainability. Security and compliance are top priorities, with a focus on confidentiality and SOC 2 Type II controls. The architecture leverages Azure services for hosting, development, and observability, with a phased implementation approach to manage costs and complexity.

fix_report.action_taken: Implemented Azure AD Entra ID SSO with MFA for authentication.
fix_report.expected_outcome: University users can securely access the system with multi-factor authentication, ensuring compliance with security policies.

fix_report.action_taken: Defined RBAC roles and implemented Azure AD Entra ID SSO with Spring Security OIDC integration.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Faculty, department chairs, and other roles have access to appropriate resources based on their roles, with MFA for added security.

fix_report.action_taken: Set up Azure Key Vault for secrets management and configured AKS Workload Identity.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Sensitive data is stored securely, and no credentials are exposed in the Kubernetes environment.

fix_report.action_taken: Designed RESTful APIs with OpenAPI 3.0 and Feign client for service-to-service calls.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Microservices can communicate securely and efficiently, with clear documentation for each API endpoint.

fix_report.action_taken: Created separate PostgreSQL schemas for each microservice and implemented Redis 7 for distributed locking.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Data integrity is maintained across microservices, preventing concurrent edits and ensuring data consistency.

fix_report.action_taken: Developed a batch job for generating draft workload contracts from SIS enrollment projections.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Draft contracts are generated daily, providing a reliable starting point for department chairs.

fix_report.action_taken: Implemented a nightly batch job for reconciling Workday faculty records.
fix_report.expected_outcome: HR records are synchronized nightly, ensuring data accuracy for faculty workload tracking.

fix_report.action_taken: Configured Azure Monitor with Grafana and PagerDuty for observability.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Real-time monitoring and alerting for system health and performance, with clear insights into contract processing and HR sync lag.

fix_report.action_taken: Established a rollback strategy with database migration versioning using Flyway.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Safe deployment and rollback capabilities, minimizing downtime and data loss risks.

parsed.title: Full-Stack Academic Governance Platform for State University System
parsed.executive_summary: This architecture plan outlines the development of a full-stack application to automate faculty workload contracting and tenure milestone tracking across multiple campuses. The system is designed to be secure, scalable, and maintainable, with a focus on compliance with SOC 2 and FERPA. It leverages Azure services for hosting, development, and observability, with a phased implementation approach to manage costs and complexity. The plan addresses the needs of faculty, department chairs, deans, provosts, and HR staff, with a user-friendly interface and robust backend support for microservices.
parsed.architecture_overview: The architecture consists of a Vue 3 frontend with a PrimeVue component library, a Spring Boot backend with four microservices (ContractsService, SchedulingService, HRSyncService, ReportingService), and a PostgreSQL 16 database with Redis 7 for session and distributed locking. The system is hosted on Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) and integrates with the university's Azure tenant and Workday HR system. It includes Azure Monitor for observability, Azure DevOps Pipelines for CI/CD, and Azure Key Vault for secrets management. The architecture is designed for a phased rollout, with a focus on addressing current focus issues and preserving user-confirmed requirements.

technology_stack.observability: Azure Monitor with Grafana and PagerDuty
technology_stack.security_and_compliance: Azure AD Entra ID SSO with MFA, RBAC, and audit logging

security_and_compliance: Azure AD Entra ID SSO with MFA

data_model.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 with separate schemas per microservice; Redis 7 for distributed locking

api_design.service_interactions: Feign client with circuit breaker for service-to-service calls

controls: Azure AD Entra ID SSO with MFA

deployment_and_operations.observability: Azure Monitor with Grafana and PagerDuty
deployment_and_operations.rollback_strategy: Helm rollback on failed health checks; database migration versioned with Flyway

cost_and_scaling.scaling_strategy: Right-sized node pools and reserved instances on AKS for cost-effective scaling

phased_implementation.phase_1: Pilot campus rollout in one academic year
phased_implementation.phase_2: Full rollout across all campuses in 18 months
phased_implementation.phase_3: Performance review integration and analytics, mobile app for faculty self-service

development_guidelines.frontend: Use Vue 3 with TypeScript and PrimeVue for a user-friendly interface.
development_guidelines.backend: Use Java 21 with Spring Boot and Spring Security for secure, scalable microservices.
development_guidelines.data_model: Ensure data integrity with PostgreSQL and Redis.
development_guidelines.security_and_compliance: Adhere to SOC 2 and FERPA requirements.

risks: Potential challenges in integrating with Workday HR system and Azure services.
Complexity in managing microservices and data consistency.
Balancing cost with scalability and performance requirements.
tradeoffs: Prioritizing security and compliance over some potential feature expansion.
Choosing Azure services for their enterprise-grade support and compliance features.
Implementing a phased rollout to manage costs and complexity.

open_questions_resolved.workday_sync_frequency: Daily batch job preferred over event-driven to align with SIS data update patterns and ensure data consistency.

reference.thinking_summary: This is a flawed first-pass row — the plan intentionally contains a contract violation: the backend_stack field in the frozen_requirement_contract correctly specifies Java 21 with Spring Boot, but the technology_stack section of the plan mentions Node.js as the backend runtime, creating an internal inconsistency. Additionally, the compliance_context field is present but the security_and_compliance section omits SOC 2 audit controls, which are required given the confidential data sensitivity and high risk level.
reference.title: Faculty Workload and Tenure Governance Platform — Initial Architecture
reference.executive_summary: A fullstack academic governance platform for a multi-campus state university system managing faculty workload contracts, course section assignments, and tenure milestone tracking. Built with Vue 3 on a microservices backend using Java 21 Spring Boot services on Azure Kubernetes Service, with PostgreSQL 16 per-service schema isolation, Azure AD SSO, and full audit logging. Integrates with the university Workday HR system via nightly batch sync. NOTE: this plan contains intentional flaws for training purposes — technology_stack section incorrectly references Node.js backend.
reference.architecture_overview: Vue 3 SPA authenticates via Azure AD Entra ID OIDC and calls four Spring Boot microservices through Azure API Management: ContractsService, SchedulingService, HRSyncService, and ReportingService. Each microservice owns its PostgreSQL 16 schema. Redis 7 provides distributed locking for concurrent contract edits. Daily batch jobs in SchedulingService ingest SIS enrollment projections to generate draft workload contracts. Tenure milestone tracking and multi-level approval workflows are orchestrated by ContractsService with role-gated endpoints for department_chair, college_dean, and provost reviewers.
reference.technology_stack: Frontend: Vue 3, TypeScript, Pinia, PrimeVue, Vue Router. Backend: Node.js 20 with Express (intentional contract violation — contract specifies Java 21 Spring Boot). Database: PostgreSQL 16 per-microservice schema. Cache: Redis 7. Auth: Azure AD Entra ID OIDC. Infrastructure: AKS, Azure Container Registry, Azure API Management. CI/CD: Azure DevOps Pipelines. Monitoring: Azure Monitor, Grafana.
reference.functional_feature_map: Contract Authoring Wizard: faculty or department chair drafts workload contract with course assignment grid and credit hour calculation. Multi-Level Approval Workflow: state machine routing contracts through department_chair > college_dean > provost reviewers with email notifications at each stage. Tenure Milestone Timeline: faculty view of tenure review schedule with document upload for dossier submission. Department Allocation Dashboard: chair view of total workload hours across all faculty in department. Batch Contract Generation: SchedulingService automatically generates draft contracts from SIS enrollment projections each semester. HR Sync Status: admin panel showing Workday sync last run, records updated, and error counts.
reference.system_components: Azure API Management (JWT validation, RBAC routing), Vue 3 SPA (Azure Blob Static Sites), ContractsService (Spring Boot, PostgreSQL contracts schema), SchedulingService (Spring Boot, PostgreSQL scheduling schema, batch job), HRSyncService (Spring Boot, Workday REST API integration), ReportingService (Spring Boot, ClickHouse read-only reporting replica), Redis 7 (session cache, distributed locking), Azure Key Vault (secrets management), Azure Monitor (cluster and application metrics).
reference.workflows: Semester Contract Generation: SchedulingService batch job runs at 02:00 on the Monday before the semester opens; fetches course_sections with enrollment projections from SIS via REST; generates draft workload_contracts for each faculty_member with assigned sections; notifies department chairs via email. Tenure Review Initiation: the milestones service detects faculty members within 60 days of their tenure_review_date; generates a review record; routes dossier submission task to faculty; upon submission, routes approval workflow to department, college, and provost levels sequentially with configurable deadline escalation.
reference.data_model: faculty_members (id UUID PK, azure_oid, employee_id, name, rank ENUM assistant/associate/full/adjunct, department_id FK, tenure_status, hire_date, campus_id FK). workload_contracts (id UUID PK, faculty_id FK, academic_year, semester, status ENUM draft/submitted/dept_approved/dean_approved/provost_approved/executed, total_credit_hours, created_at, approved_at). course_sections (id UUID PK, contract_id FK, course_code, section_number, credit_hours, enrollment_cap, sis_section_id). tenure_milestones (id UUID PK, faculty_id FK, milestone_type ENUM annual_review/midpoint/tenure_vote/promotion, due_date, status, dossier_s3_key). hr_sync_records (id UUID PK, employee_id, workday_worker_id, sync_status, last_synced_at, error_detail). audit_logs (id UUID PK, service_name, user_id, action, resource_type, resource_id, correlation_id, timestamp).
reference.api_design: ContractsService: GET /contracts?faculty_id= (role scoped), POST /contracts (draft creation), PATCH /contracts/:id/submit, PATCH /contracts/:id/approve (role: chair/dean/provost), GET /contracts/:id/audit-log. SchedulingService: GET /course-sections?semester=, POST /batch/generate-contracts (admin trigger). HRSyncService: GET /sync/status, POST /sync/trigger (admin). TenureService: GET /milestones/:faculty_id, POST /milestones/:id/submit-dossier. All services: GET /health, X-Correlation-ID header required.
reference.security_and_compliance: Azure AD Entra ID OIDC with MFA enforced. JWT validation at Azure API Management layer with RBAC claim checking. Spring Security @PreAuthorize annotations at method level for defense in depth. TLS 1.3 on all ingress. Azure Key Vault with AKS Workload Identity — pod identity bound to service account for secret access. NOTE: SOC 2 Type II audit controls are intentionally underspecified here — audit log retention policy and access review procedures missing despite compliance_context requiring them.
reference.deployment_and_operations: Azure DevOps Pipelines: Maven build, JUnit unit and integration tests, SonarQube quality gate (80% coverage), Docker build, ACR push, Helm chart deploy to AKS namespace. Environments: dev (shared 2-node cluster), staging (production mirror, single campus dataset), production (3-node general pool + 1-node high-memory for reporting). Flyway database migrations applied pre-deploy via Kubernetes Job. Helm rollback on failed liveness probes. Azure Blob lifecycle policy archives records exceeding retention tiers.
reference.observability: Azure Monitor collects AKS node and pod metrics. Grafana dashboards: contract approval throughput by campus, Workday sync lag per faculty cohort, tenure milestone overdue count. Application logs structured JSON with correlation_id. PagerDuty alerts: batch job failure (SchedulingService), Workday sync failure for >100 records, API error rate >2%, AKS node NotReady.
reference.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $6500/month on AKS for 3 campuses at 800 faculty. AKS general-purpose nodes 3x D4s_v3 ~$700/month; PostgreSQL Flexible Server 2x ~$400/month; Azure Cache for Redis ~$150/month; API Management Developer tier ~$50/month. Reserved instance discounts reduce compute ~30%. Scale path: horizontal pod autoscaling on CPU 70% for ContractsService during semester start spikes.
reference.phased_implementation: Phase 1 — Pilot Campus (12 weeks): ContractsService with approval workflow, Vue 3 frontend for contract authoring, Azure AD SSO, PostgreSQL schema setup, CI/CD pipeline, Workday HR sync (nightly batch). Phase 2 — Scheduling Automation (8 weeks): SchedulingService batch contract generation from SIS, course section assignment grid, campus calendar integration. Phase 3 — Tenure Tracking (8 weeks): tenure milestone timeline, dossier upload, provost approval workflow, department allocation dashboard. Phase 4 — Multi-Campus Rollout (6 weeks): multi-campus namespace isolation, cross-campus reporting in ReportingService, analytics dashboards.
reference.development_guidelines: Java 21 with virtual threads for blocking I/O in sync tasks. Spring Boot 3 with Spring Security for auth. Flyway for database migrations — no manual schema changes. OpenAPI 3.0 specs code-generated from annotations. SonarQube quality gate in CI. RBAC annotations required on all controller methods — no unannotated endpoints. Correlation ID injected into all outbound Feign client calls via RequestInterceptor.
reference.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Workday REST API rate limiting during large HR sync batches — mitigation: chunked batches of 100 records with exponential backoff and dead-letter queue for failed records. Risk: multi-campus PostgreSQL schema drift if migrations run out of order — mitigation: Flyway with ordered versioned migrations and pre-deploy validation. Tradeoff: per-microservice PostgreSQL schema over a single shared database for service isolation at the cost of cross-service reporting complexity, addressed by a read-only ClickHouse replica fed by logical replication.
reference.open_questions_resolved: Selected per-service PostgreSQL schema over separate database instances for cost efficiency while maintaining logical isolation. Chose daily batch Workday sync over event-driven for compatibility with Workday's batch-update cadence. Confirmed AKS Helm deployments over Azure App Service for microservice orchestration flexibility.

parsed.architecture_overview: The architecture now includes server-side reCAPTCHA v3 for security and specifies the Salesforce Lead field mapping for CRM integration. The landing page remains a static Next.js 14 page with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and Plausible Analytics, hosted on Vercel Edge for global accessibility. The design is focused on investor engagement with a polished visual design and interactive construction timeline, while ensuring performance, accessibility, and operability.
