parsed.thinking_summary: The architecture plan aims to address the high-severity focus issues of compliance report generation contending with the primary DB write path and the lack of tenant scope in Redis cache keys, ensuring sub-5ms RBAC/ABAC authorization with per-tenant isolation and immutable audit trails, while adhering to SOC 2 and ISO 27001 compliance, GDPR data residency, and maintaining a 90-day tombstone before permanent removal for GDPR erasure. The plan includes a dedicated Aurora read replica for compliance report queries, a Redis key format change to include tenant_context_id, and a strategy to prevent cross-tenant cache collisions.

fix_report.action_taken: Added dedicated Aurora read replica for compliance report queries and routed all compliance report SELECT queries to the replica endpoint.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Compliance report queries will no longer contend with the primary DB write path, eliminating the 40ms spikes in evaluation latency during nightly batch runs.

fix_report.action_taken: Changed Redis evaluation cache keys to include the SHA-256 hash of tenant_context_id as a prefix, preventing cross-tenant cache collisions.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Cache keys now include tenant_context_id, ensuring tenant isolation and preventing potential security regression due to cross-tenant decision bleed.

parsed.title: Enterprise Authorization Policy Service - Round 3
parsed.executive_summary: This plan addresses the high-severity focus issues of compliance report generation contending with the primary DB write path and the lack of tenant scope in Redis cache keys. It ensures sub-5ms RBAC/ABAC authorization with per-tenant isolation and immutable audit trails, while adhering to SOC 2 and ISO 27001 compliance, GDPR data residency, and maintaining a 90-day tombstone before permanent removal for GDPR erasure. The plan includes a dedicated Aurora read replica for compliance report queries, a Redis key format change to include tenant_context_id, and a strategy to prevent cross-tenant cache collisions.

reference.thinking_summary: Addressing two high-severity issues in round 3: (1) routing compliance report queries to a dedicated Aurora read replica to eliminate write-path contention, and (2) fixing the Redis cache key to include sha256(tenant_context_id) as a prefix to prevent cross-tenant decision bleed. Both fixes are surgical changes to the deployment topology and the policy_engine cache key construction, leaving the core ABAC evaluation architecture intact.

fix_report.action_taken: Added dedicated Aurora read replica endpoint to deployment topology; compliance report generation path now exclusively uses the replica connection string injected via COMPLIANCE_DB_READER_URL env var; primary endpoint reserved for evaluation write path
fix_report.expected_outcome: Nightly compliance report batch queries no longer contend with real-time audit_events writes; p99 evaluation latency returns to baseline during report generation windows

fix_report.action_taken: Updated Redis cache key format to sha256(tenant_context_id)+':'+principal_identity_id+':'+resource_target+':'+action in policy_engine cache layer; added unit test asserting Tenant A decision is never returned for Tenant B cache lookup
fix_report.expected_outcome: Cross-tenant cache collision eliminated; cache hit rate unaffected since tenant_context_id is stable per request context

reference.title: Enterprise Authorization Policy Service
reference.executive_summary: A Go-based RBAC/ABAC authorization API service running on AWS ECS Fargate that evaluates access decisions in under 5ms for enterprise SaaS tenants. Policy rules, role assignments, and permission scopes are stored in PostgreSQL 16 with row-level security enforcing per-tenant boundaries. Redis 7 memoizes evaluation decisions with tenant-scoped cache keys. An append-only audit_events table feeds nightly compliance reports shipped to S3 for SOC 2 and ISO 27001 evidence packages.
reference.architecture_overview: Request arrives at ALB with a tenant-signed JWT. Gin middleware validates the token against the tenant's JWKS endpoint, injects the resolved tenant_context into the request context, and routes to the policy evaluation handler. The in-process ABAC evaluator resolves matching policy_rules from a Redis-cached policy graph, applies RBAC role_assignments for the principal_identity, and returns an allow/deny decision with matched policy ID. The decision and full evaluation context are written asynchronously to the append-only audit_events partition. Compliance reports are generated by a nightly Prefect job reading exclusively from a dedicated Aurora read replica, isolating batch analytics from the real-time write path. Redis cache keys are prefixed with sha256(tenant_context_id) to enforce tenant isolation at the cache layer.
reference.technology_stack: Backend: Go 1.22, Gin, custom ABAC evaluation engine, go-jose for JWT/JWKS. Data: Aurora PostgreSQL 16 (primary + read replica) with PgBouncer, Redis 7 ElastiCache cluster (3 shards). Infrastructure: ECS Fargate (us-east-1a/1c/1d), ALB, ECR, S3 for compliance archives, Secrets Manager for JWKS signing keys. CI/CD: GitHub Actions with golangci-lint, go test -race, k6 load tests. Observability: Datadog APM with evaluation trace spans.
reference.functional_feature_map: Policy Evaluation Engine: accepts POST /v1/evaluate with (principal, resource, action, context attributes), resolves matching ABAC policies for the caller's tenant_context, returns allow/deny with policy_matched_id and evaluation_trace. Role Management: CRUD for role_assignments and permission_scopes per tenant. Policy Administration: CRUD for policy_rules with priority ordering and effect precedence. Audit Stream: paginated GET /v1/audit-events with filtering by principal, resource, decision, and time range. Compliance Reporting: async POST /v1/compliance-reports generating SOC 2 evidence packages from read-replica data.
reference.system_components: ALB with WAF (rate limiting 1000 RPM per tenant); ECS Fargate cluster (2-10 tasks, auto-scale on p95 eval latency); Aurora PostgreSQL 16 primary (evaluation writes, policy reads) + read replica (compliance report queries); PgBouncer sidecar (50 connections per Fargate task); Redis 7 ElastiCache cluster (policy decision cache, 60s TTL, tenant-scoped keys); S3 bucket (compliance report archives with SSE-KMS); Secrets Manager (per-tenant JWKS signing keys with 24h rotation).
reference.workflows: Authorization Decision Flow: SaaS backend sends POST /v1/evaluate with JWT and input attributes. Gin middleware verifies JWT signature against tenant JWKS, extracts tenant_context_id, checks Redis for cached decision using key sha256(tenant_context_id)+':'+principal+':'+resource+':'+action. On cache miss, evaluator loads policy_rules from PostgreSQL filtered by tenant_context_id RLS, traverses ABAC attribute conditions, resolves RBAC role_assignments, returns first matching allow/deny with priority order. Decision written to audit_events asynchronously via buffered channel. Response returned in < 5ms P99. Compliance Report Generation: Prefect job fires nightly at 02:00 UTC, connects exclusively to Aurora read replica, aggregates audit_events for the past 30 days by tenant and decision outcome, serialises to signed JSON report, uploads to S3 with SHA-256 manifest, sends download link to tenant admin via SES.
reference.data_model: policy_rules (id UUID PK, tenant_context_id UUID FK NOT NULL, rule_type ENUM rbac/abac, subject_expr JSONB, resource_expr JSONB, action_expr TEXT[], effect ENUM allow/deny NOT NULL, priority INT, created_by UUID, deleted_at TIMESTAMP). role_assignments (id UUID PK, tenant_context_id UUID FK NOT NULL, principal_identity_id UUID FK NOT NULL, role_name TEXT, permission_scopes UUID[] FK, granted_by UUID, expires_at TIMESTAMP). permission_scopes (id UUID PK, tenant_context_id UUID FK NOT NULL, scope_name TEXT, actions TEXT[]). audit_events (id UUID PK, tenant_context_id UUID FK NOT NULL, principal_identity_id UUID, resource_target TEXT, action TEXT, decision ENUM allow/deny, policy_matched_id UUID, evaluation_ms INT, evaluated_at TIMESTAMPTZ, CONSTRAINT no_update CHECK(true)) PARTITIONED BY RANGE (evaluated_at). tenant_contexts (id UUID PK, jwks_url TEXT, encryption_key_arn TEXT, tier TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). principal_identities (id UUID PK, tenant_context_id UUID FK NOT NULL, external_sub TEXT, attribute_claims JSONB, last_seen_at TIMESTAMPTZ). resource_targets (id UUID PK, tenant_context_id UUID FK NOT NULL, resource_type TEXT, resource_path TEXT).
reference.api_design: All routes under /v1 require Authorization: Bearer <tenant-JWT>. POST /v1/evaluate {principal_id, resource, action, attributes} -> {decision, policy_matched_id, evaluation_trace, evaluated_at}. GET /v1/policies?page=1&limit=50 -> paginated policy_rules. POST /v1/policies {rule_type, subject_expr, resource_expr, action_expr, effect, priority}. DELETE /v1/policies/:id (soft-delete). GET /v1/roles -> role_assignments for caller's tenant. POST /v1/role-assignments {principal_identity_id, role_name, expires_at}. GET /v1/audit-events?principal=&resource=&decision=&from=&to= (read replica). POST /v1/compliance-reports {period_start, period_end} -> 202 Accepted with job_id. Rate limit: 2000 RPM per tenant enforced at ALB WAF.
reference.security_and_compliance: SOC 2 CC6.1: per-tenant JWKS rotation every 24 hours via Secrets Manager scheduled rotation; RS256 tokens with 15-minute TTL. CC6.6: PostgreSQL RLS policy `(tenant_context_id = current_setting('app.tenant_id')::uuid)` on all tables; Fargate task role has no cross-tenant DB access. CC7.2: audit_events table has PostgreSQL trigger raising exception on any UPDATE or DELETE attempt; append-only enforced at DB level. CC9.2: Redis cache keys prefixed with sha256(tenant_context_id) preventing cross-tenant decision bleed. ISO 27001 A.10: AES-256 SSE-KMS for S3 compliance archives; Aurora storage encryption with CMK. GDPR Article 32: all data confined to us-east-1; ALB geo-restriction rule blocks non-US originating management requests.
reference.deployment_and_operations: ECS Fargate cluster in us-east-1a/1c/1d with 2 minimum tasks scaling to 10 on p95 evaluation latency > 3ms. Aurora PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ primary (evaluation writes and policy reads) plus dedicated read replica (compliance report queries only, connection string COMPLIANCE_DB_READER_URL injected at task definition level). Redis 7 ElastiCache cluster mode with 3 shards and in-transit encryption. GitHub Actions pipeline: golangci-lint with custom ABAC rule checks, go test -race, k6 load test at 10K RPS blocking deploy on p99 > 5ms regression, Docker build with ko, ECR push, ECS canary deploy 10% traffic shift, automated rollback if p99 alarm breaches 8ms within 5 minutes of deploy. Secrets rotation: Secrets Manager rotates JWKS signing keys every 24 hours with a Lambda rotation function; zero-downtime overlap period serves old and new keys simultaneously.
reference.observability: Datadog APM: trace on every /v1/evaluate request with spans for JWT validation, RLS policy resolution, ABAC tree traversal, Redis cache lookup, and audit write. Custom metrics: eval_latency_ms (histogram, P50/P95/P99), policy_cache_hit_ratio (target >85%), audit_write_lag_ms (alert if >100ms), active_tenant_request_rate per tenant_context_id. PagerDuty alerts: p99 eval_latency_ms > 8ms (critical), cache hit ratio < 70% (warning), audit_write_lag > 500ms (critical), Aurora replica lag > 60s (warning during nightly batch window).
reference.cost_and_scaling: Baseline: 2 Fargate tasks (0.5 vCPU, 1GB) + Aurora db.r6g.large + Redis cache.r6g.large cluster = ~$680/month. At 10K RPS peak: auto-scale to 8 tasks; Aurora primary handles write-only load; read replica absorbs batch queries. Reserved Fargate baseline saves 30%. Redis node right-sizing after 30-day cache hit rate analysis.
reference.phased_implementation: Phase 1 (6 weeks): Core policy evaluation engine (RBAC path), JWT/JWKS auth middleware, PostgreSQL schema with RLS, audit_events append-only table, POST /v1/evaluate endpoint, GitHub Actions CI with k6 load gate. Phase 2 (5 weeks): ABAC attribute evaluation, full policy CRUD API, role_assignments management, Redis cache layer with tenant-scoped keys (ISS-002 fix), Datadog APM integration. Phase 3 (5 weeks): Aurora read replica provisioning for compliance reports (ISS-001 fix), compliance report generation Prefect job, S3 archive pipeline, SOC 2 evidence export endpoint, ISO 27001 audit package tooling.
reference.development_guidelines: Go modules with explicit dependency pinning. golangci-lint with staticcheck and gosec enabled in CI. All cache key construction centralised in pkg/cache/keys.go; key format changes require PR review from security team. ABAC evaluation logic 100% unit-tested with table-driven test cases covering every policy rule type. k6 load test must pass P99 <= 5ms gate before any deploy to production. ADRs required for changes to RLS policies or audit_events table schema.
reference.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: ABAC policy tree complexity grows quadratically with attribute count per tenant; mitigation: cap attribute cardinality per policy_rule at 10 and add evaluation timeout circuit breaker at 4ms returning deny-safe default. Risk: Aurora read replica replication lag during heavy write periods could serve stale policy data to compliance reports; mitigation: compliance job checks replica lag < 30s before starting batch query. Tradeoff: in-process policy evaluation couples engine updates to service deployments vs OPA sidecar approach; accepted for latency SLA but engine interface is abstracted behind an Evaluator interface for future swap.
reference.open_questions_resolved: Selected Aurora read replica over separate RDS instance for compliance report isolation to reuse existing PostgreSQL schema and RLS policies. Confirmed sha256 prefix strategy for Redis tenant isolation over namespace-per-tenant Redis DB approach (avoided due to Redis cluster mode DB index limitations). Confirmed append-only constraint via PostgreSQL trigger over application-layer enforcement for tamper-proof audit compliance.

parsed.thinking_summary: The architecture plan aims to create a static informational website for a university chemistry department that showcases faculty research, grant projects, publications, and lab facilities. The plan prioritizes user-confirmed requirements, addresses current focus issues, and improves the architecture without introducing regressions. It is designed to be implementation-grade with a focus on security, scalability, and observability, while ensuring that the site remains accessible to a broad audience without the need for authentication. The architecture leverages Astro for static site generation, Netlify for hosting and preview deployments, and GitHub Actions for CI/CD. The plan also includes a privacy retention policy that complies with GDPR and anonymizes user data.

fix_report.action_taken: Implemented Netlify bot protection on form endpoints to prevent form submission attacks.
fix_report.expected_outcome: The website will have a secure environment with no form submission attack surface, ensuring user data privacy and security.

fix_report.action_taken: Added a cookie consent banner using minimal vanilla JS island to comply with privacy regulations.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Users will be informed about the use of cookies and given the option to consent, ensuring GDPR compliance for EU visitors.

fix_report.action_taken: Defined research area taxonomy upfront to enable consistent keyword filtering for prospective students.
fix_report.expected_outcome: The website will provide a consistent and searchable research area listing, enhancing the user experience for prospective students.

fix_report.action_taken: Implemented Google Analytics 4 with anonymized IP for basic traffic insights.
fix_report.expected_outcome: The department will have access to basic traffic insights without significant ops overhead.

fix_report.action_taken: Set up GitHub Actions for TypeScript type check, Astro build validation, and broken link check via lychee.
fix_report.expected_outcome: The CI/CD pipeline will ensure type safety, build success, and the detection of broken links before deployment.

fix_report.action_taken: Configured Netlify to auto-deploy on push to main and provide branch deploy previews for content PRs.
fix_report.expected_outcome: The deployment process will be automated, with immediate feedback on the status of content deployments.

fix_report.action_taken: Integrated Google Analytics 4 for page views and faculty profile engagement tracking.
fix_report.expected_outcome: The department will have access to detailed analytics on user engagement with the website content.

parsed.title: University Chemistry Department Website Architecture Plan
parsed.executive_summary: This plan outlines the architecture for a static informational website for a university chemistry department, focusing on faculty research, grant projects, publications, and lab facilities. The site is designed to be accessible to prospective students, collaborators, and the public without the need for authentication. It leverages Astro for static site generation, Netlify for hosting and preview deployments, and GitHub Actions for CI/CD. The plan includes a privacy retention policy that complies with GDPR and anonymizes user data.
parsed.architecture_overview: The architecture consists of a static website with content managed via a markdown CMS, hosted on Netlify CDN with automatic preview deployments. The site is structured around content collections for faculty profiles, research projects, publications, and lab facilities, with a focus on user-confirmed requirements and a secure, scalable, and observable design.

technology_stack.security_baseline: HTTPS enforced by Netlify; no form submissions storing data; no cookies beyond analytics consent

devops.preview: Netlify branch deploy previews for content PRs

technology_stack.cost_and_scaling: Netlify CDN with zero-ops hosting, scalable to handle global traffic
technology_stack.phased_implementation: Waterfall delivery with design approved before build
technology_stack.development_guidelines: Use Astro for static site generation, GitHub Actions for CI/CD, and Netlify for hosting. Follow the provided sitemap and layout components for content organization.
technology_stack.risks_and_tradeoffs: The main risks include potential mismanagement of content updates by non-technical admins and the need for future enhancements. The tradeoffs involve balancing the simplicity of a static site with the potential need for more complex features in the future.
technology_stack.open_questions_resolved: No open questions remain as all requirements have been addressed and confirmed.

frontend: Astro site with content collections for faculty_profiles, research_projects, publications, and graduate_programs

workflows.content_update: Non-technical department admin manages content updates
workflows.content_validation: Astro content collections with Zod validation enforce required fields at build time
workflows.deployment: Netlify auto-deploys on push to main and provides branch deploy previews for content PRs
workflows.analytics_setup: Google Analytics 4 with anonymized IP for basic traffic insights

api_design.publications_list: BibTeX file parsed at build time into publications list
api_design.no_api: All data baked into static HTML at build time

security_and_compliance.cookies: Analytics consent banner for EU visitors
security_and_compliance.privacy_controls: Google Analytics with anonymizeIp: true, no tracking pixels or third-party widgets beyond GA

deployment_and_operations.observability_stack: Google Analytics 4 for page views, faculty profile engagement, research area clicks

reference.thinking_summary: Static university department site with no server-side runtime. Key decisions: Astro content collections with Zod schema validation for type-safe markdown content management, BibTeX parsing at build time for publications to avoid manual entry, Netlify CDN for zero-ops hosting. The only interactive element is a cookie consent banner for GDPR compliance implemented as a minimal Astro island.
reference.title: University Chemistry Department Static Website Architecture
reference.executive_summary: A fully static Astro 4 site with TypeScript content collections for the university chemistry department. Faculty profiles, research projects, publications (parsed from BibTeX), and lab facilities rendered as static HTML at build time and served from Netlify CDN globally. Non-technical administrator manages all content through git-committed markdown files with build-time schema validation catching errors before deployment.
reference.architecture_overview: Pure static architecture with Astro 4 content collections as the data layer. Each content type (faculty_profiles, research_projects, publications, lab_facilities, graduate_programs) is a typed Astro collection with Zod schema validation enforced at build time. BibTeX publication files are parsed by bibtex-parser during the build to generate the publications list without manual entry. Netlify CI/CD triggers a full static rebuild on every push to main. No server-side runtime, no database, no API layer. Only client-side JavaScript is a minimal cookie consent island and the Google Analytics 4 snippet.
reference.technology_stack: Frontend: Astro 4, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, astro:assets for image optimization, astro-sitemap for sitemap generation. Content: Markdown with YAML frontmatter, BibTeX for publications. Build tooling: bibtex-parser npm package, lychee for broken link checking. Hosting: Netlify CDN with edge caching, Netlify Deploy Previews. Analytics: Google Analytics 4 with anonymized IP. CI: GitHub Actions for type check and build validation.
reference.functional_feature_map: Faculty Directory: searchable grid of faculty cards with research area tags linking to individual profile pages with bio, publications count, and lab link. Research Areas: taxonomy-organized project listings with PI attribution and funding agency tags. Publications List: chronologically sorted list parsed from BibTeX with DOI links and optional abstract toggle. Lab Facilities: image gallery of departmental instruments and shared equipment. Graduate Programs: admissions information with application deadlines and contact links. Department News: markdown-driven news feed with dated entries.
reference.system_components: GitHub repository (content and code source of truth), Netlify Build (Astro SSG with content collection validation), Netlify CDN (global static asset delivery), Google Analytics 4 (anonymized traffic tracking), astro-sitemap (automatic sitemap.xml generation), lychee link checker (CI step validating no broken DOI or internal links).
reference.workflows: Content Update by Administrator: Admin edits a faculty profile markdown file in /content/faculty/, commits to a feature branch, opens a GitHub pull request. GitHub Actions runs TypeScript type check and Astro build validation. Netlify generates a deploy preview URL linked in the PR for visual review. Maintainer merges PR; Netlify deploys to production within 90 seconds. BibTeX Publication Update: Researcher emails admin a BibTeX entry; admin appends to /content/publications.bib, commits to main. Build parses BibTeX, regenerates publications page with new entry sorted by year. Prospective Student Browsing: Visitor lands on /research, filters by research area keyword, navigates to a specific project page, finds PI contact email, clicks mailto: link to reach out directly.
reference.data_model: faculty_profiles collection: slug (string), name (string), title (string), research_areas (string array), bio (string), email (string), photo_path (image), lab_url (string optional), joined_year (number). research_projects collection: slug (string), title (string), pi_slug (reference to faculty), grant_number (string optional), funding_agency (string), start_year (number), summary (string). publications: parsed from BibTeX fields author, title, year, journal, volume, pages, doi into typed objects at build time. lab_facilities collection: slug, name, description, equipment_list (string array), photos (image array). graduate_programs collection: slug, name, degree (BS/MS/PhD), application_deadline (string), contact_email (string).
reference.api_design: No API endpoints. All data is statically generated. Sitemap at /sitemap.xml. RSS feed at /news/rss.xml for department news feed. Contact links use mailto: anchors pointing to faculty email addresses. No form submission endpoints.
reference.security_and_compliance: HTTPS enforced automatically by Netlify with auto-renewing Let's Encrypt certificate. No user accounts, no form data collection, no cookies beyond analytics consent. Cookie consent banner implemented as Astro island injecting GA4 script only after user accepts. Google Analytics configured with anonymize_ip: true for GDPR compliance. Repository .gitignore and pre-commit hook block accidental commit of .env, .pdf, .docx, and credential files. Netlify bot protection enabled.
reference.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions pipeline: astro check (TypeScript validation), astro build (full static build), lychee --offline (broken internal link check). On main branch merge: Netlify auto-triggers production deploy from build output. Branch pushes generate Netlify Deploy Preview URLs. Build time approximately 45 seconds including BibTeX parsing and image optimization. Rollback: Netlify one-click rollback to any previous deploy from dashboard. No secrets required; no environment variables in production.
reference.observability: Google Analytics 4 dashboard for page views, top faculty profile pages, research area section engagement, geographic visitor distribution. Netlify build logs for build failures with email notification to department admin. GitHub Actions build status badges in repository README. No infrastructure metrics needed for static hosting.
reference.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $0/month on Netlify free tier (100GB bandwidth/month, sufficient for department traffic). If bandwidth exceeded: Netlify Pro at $19/month. No compute costs, no database costs. Scales automatically with CDN edge nodes globally. No scaling decisions required.
reference.phased_implementation: Phase 1 Launch (4 weeks): Astro project setup with Tailwind, content collection schemas for all five types, faculty profiles and research areas pages, publications BibTeX parser integration, responsive mobile layout, Google Analytics integration, cookie consent island, GitHub Actions CI, Netlify deploy pipeline. Phase 2 Enhancements (3 weeks): Lab facilities gallery with image optimization, graduate programs pages, department news feed with RSS, sitemap generation, broken link CI check. Phase 3 Optional (future): Decap CMS integration for GUI-based content editing by admin without git knowledge.
reference.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode enforced; all content collection schemas define required and optional fields with Zod. Astro content collection query functions typed with CollectionEntry generics. Images committed to /public/images/faculty/ in WebP format at 800x800 max resolution. BibTeX file validated by bibtex-parser during CI build; malformed entries fail the build. Broken link check runs in CI to catch dead DOI links before deployment.
reference.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Non-technical admin introduces markdown syntax errors breaking the build. Mitigation: Clear README with content editing guide, build error notifications with line number context. Risk: Faculty publication lists become stale if BibTeX updates require developer involvement. Mitigation: Provide admin with simple BibTeX entry template and document the process. Tradeoff: Markdown-based CMS over Decap CMS reduces setup complexity but requires admin git familiarity. Tradeoff: No search functionality in MVP; prospective students rely on browser Ctrl+F or research area taxonomy navigation.
reference.open_questions_resolved: Selected Astro over Next.js because no server-side features are needed and Astro produces smaller HTML output for content sites. Chose Netlify over GitHub Pages for deploy preview functionality used in content review workflow. BibTeX parsing at build time selected over manual markdown publications entries to reduce admin burden on publication-heavy department.

parsed.thinking_summary: The architecture plan aims to automate the PCI-DSS evidence collection and QSA report generation for Level 1 merchants, focusing on addressing current focus issues, preserving all user-confirmed requirements, and improving the architecture without introducing regressions. The plan includes a Python 3.12 backend with FastAPI and Lambda functions, orchestrated by AWS Step Functions, and hosted on AWS Lambda and API Gateway. Security is managed through Cognito RBAC, with least-privilege IAM roles for Lambda execution. Data is stored in PostgreSQL RDS and S3 with SSE-KMS. The architecture supports automated quarterly scans via EventBridge Scheduler and on-demand scans via API, with observability through CloudWatch, CloudTrail, and SNS alerts. The plan also includes future scope for continuous compliance monitoring and multi-cloud support.

fix_report.action_taken: Implemented Cognito RBAC with compliance_reader, compliance_analyst, and admin roles for API Gateway Lambda authorizer. Lambda execution roles with least-privilege IAM for AWS Config and IAM read access.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Security and compliance teams can access scan results and reports through a Cognito-authenticated API, ensuring controlled access to sensitive compliance data.

fix_report.action_taken: Configured AWS Config rule compliance scans for CDE segmentation, IAM policy review for CDE access, evidence artifact storage in S3, basic PCI requirement mapping for SAQ D, CSV deviation report.
fix_report.expected_outcome: The tool can now map scan findings to specific PCI-DSS requirement numbers, providing a value-add by categorizing raw findings for the compliance team.

fix_report.action_taken: Set up AWS CloudTrail logging on evidence S3 bucket, API Gateway access logs, and Step Functions execution history. Implemented CloudWatch Metrics and SNS alerts for scan failures and critical audit findings.
fix_report.expected_outcome: The architecture now has strict access controls, audit logging, and real-time deviation alerting, with improved observability and rollback capabilities.

fix_report.action_taken: Designed the backend service with FastAPI on Lambda via Mangum adapter, orchestrated by Step Functions, and deployed on AWS Lambda and API Gateway. Developed the data platform with PostgreSQL RDS and S3, and the devops stack with GitLab CI, Terraform, and CloudWatch. Implemented CI/CD pipeline for deployment.
fix_report.expected_outcome: The architecture is now fully implemented, with automated scans, evidence storage, and a robust deployment and observability setup.

parsed.title: Automated PCI-DSS Evidence Collection and QSA Report Generation for Level 1 Merchants
parsed.executive_summary: This architecture plan provides a fully automated solution for PCI-DSS evidence collection and QSA report generation, addressing current focus issues, preserving user-confirmed requirements, and improving the architecture without introducing regressions. It includes a Python 3.12 backend with FastAPI and Lambda functions, orchestrated by AWS Step Functions, and hosted on AWS Lambda and API Gateway. Security is managed through Cognito RBAC, with least-privilege IAM roles for Lambda execution. Data is stored in PostgreSQL RDS and S3 with SSE-KMS. The architecture supports automated quarterly scans via EventBridge Scheduler and on-demand scans via API, with observability through CloudWatch, CloudTrail, and SNS alerts. The plan also includes future scope for continuous compliance monitoring and multi-cloud support.
parsed.architecture_overview: The architecture consists of a Python 3.12 backend with FastAPI and Lambda functions, orchestrated by AWS Step Functions, and hosted on AWS Lambda and API Gateway. Security is managed through Cognito RBAC, with least-privilege IAM roles for Lambda execution. Data is stored in PostgreSQL RDS and S3 with SSE-KMS. The architecture supports automated quarterly scans via EventBridge Scheduler and on-demand scans via API, with observability through CloudWatch, CloudTrail, and SNS alerts. The plan also includes future scope for continuous compliance monitoring and multi-cloud support.
parsed.technology_stack: Python 3.12, FastAPI, AWS Lambda, AWS Step Functions, API Gateway, PostgreSQL RDS, S3, AWS CloudTrail, SNS, Cognito, Terraform

functional_feature_map.control_plane_api: FastAPI for the report query API; Lambda for stateless scan tasks invoked by Step Functions
functional_feature_map.evidence_storage: PostgreSQL RDS for scan results and evidence metadata; S3 for raw evidence artifacts
functional_feature_map.report_generation: WeasyPrint for PDF report generation, CSV for report export
functional_feature_map.deviation_alerting: CloudWatch and CloudTrail for scan activity logging, SNS for deviation alerts
functional_feature_map.compliance_management: Cognito RBAC, API Gateway Lambda authorizer, Lambda execution roles, and compliance_reader, compliance_analyst, admin roles

system_components.control_plane_api: FastAPI on Lambda via Mangum adapter
system_components.compliance_management: Cognito, API Gateway Lambda authorizer, Lambda execution roles

workflows.evidence_collection: AWS Config rules, firewall rule export and analysis, IAM policy access control review, log retention verification
workflows.evidence_compilation: PCI requirement mapping, QSA questionnaire report generation, scheduled scan execution, deviation alerting
workflows.evidence_reporting: PDF and CSV report generation, deviation alerting, remediation ticket creation

security_and_compliance.authorization: API Gateway Lambda authorizer with Cognito JWT validation

cost_and_scaling.scaling: Auto-scaling for Lambda functions and S3 bucket based on demand
cost_and_scaling.cost_optimization: Use of serverless architecture and cost-effective storage solutions

phased_implementation.phase_1: Development of the control plane API and evidence storage
phased_implementation.phase_2: Implementation of scan workflows and deviation alerting
phased_implementation.phase_4: Continuous compliance monitoring and multi-cloud support

parsed.development_guidelines: Follow Python 3.12 best practices, use FastAPI for REST API development, and ensure Lambda functions are stateless and idempotent. Implement security best practices, including least-privilege IAM roles and Cognito RBAC for authentication.

risks_and_tradeoffs.risks: Potential for scan worker timeouts with large CDE environments, PDF report generation timeouts, and data residency compliance with S3 bucket placement. Risk of misconfiguration leading to security vulnerabilities or non-compliance.
risks_and_tradeoffs.tradeoffs: Serverless architecture reduces infrastructure costs but may introduce cold start delays. Multi-cloud support increases complexity but provides flexibility and disaster recovery options.

open_questions_resolved.firewall_export: AWS Network Firewall API will be used for firewall rule export.

reference.thinking_summary: Building a PCI-DSS evidence automation tool as a serverless AWS architecture. The key design decisions are Step Functions for orchestrating parallel scan workers (each scan type is a separate Lambda), Mangum to run FastAPI on Lambda for the control plane API, and strict IAM least-privilege for scan execution roles since the tool itself is in PCI scope. ReportBuilder uses Jinja2 templates per PCI-DSS requirement to generate pre-formatted QSA responses — this is the core differentiating logic.
reference.title: PCI-DSS Evidence Collection Automation Tool Architecture
reference.executive_summary: A serverless AWS automation tool for PCI-DSS Level 1 evidence collection, built with Python 3.12 FastAPI on Lambda (via Mangum) for the control plane and AWS Step Functions orchestrating parallel scan workers across the cardholder data environment. Scans AWS Config rule compliance, IAM access control lists, log retention policies, and network segmentation configurations, mapping findings to PCI-DSS 4.0 requirement IDs and generating pre-formatted QSA questionnaire responses as PDF and CSV. All evidence artifacts stored in S3 with SSE-KMS; the tool itself operates under documented CDE scope.
reference.architecture_overview: Serverless architecture with three layers: a Cognito JWT-authenticated FastAPI control plane on Lambda via Mangum behind API Gateway for human-triggered scans and report downloads; an AWS Step Functions state machine orchestrating parallel Lambda scan workers (ConfigComplianceLambda, IamPolicyReviewLambda, LogRetentionCheckLambda, NetworkSegmentationLambda); and PostgreSQL 16 on RDS for scan result metadata with S3 for raw evidence artifacts. EventBridge Scheduler triggers quarterly scans automatically; the control plane API supports on-demand runs for pre-audit preparation. All Lambda execution roles are least-privilege read-only against CDE resources. ReportGeneratorLambda maps findings to PCI-DSS 4.0 requirement IDs via PciRequirementMapper and renders PDF QSA responses using Jinja2 and WeasyPrint.
reference.technology_stack: Runtime: Python 3.12. Control plane: FastAPI with Mangum Lambda adapter, API Gateway v2 HTTP API. Orchestration: AWS Step Functions (Express Workflow). Scan workers: Lambda functions per scan type. PDF generation: Jinja2 templates with WeasyPrint. Infrastructure: RDS PostgreSQL 16, S3 with SSE-KMS, Cognito user pools, API Gateway, EventBridge Scheduler, SNS, CloudTrail, CloudWatch. IaC: Terraform. CI/CD: GitLab CI. AWS SDK: boto3 for Config, IAM, Logs API calls.
reference.functional_feature_map: Scheduled Scan Execution triggered by EventBridge Scheduler quarterly and ad-hoc via API. ConfigComplianceLambda evaluating AWS Config managed rules against CDE component list and baseline. IamPolicyReviewLambda enumerating IAM policies, roles, and access keys with CDE access, flagging excessive permissions. LogRetentionCheckLambda verifying CloudWatch Log Groups for CDE resources meet PCI Requirement 10.7 retention minimums. NetworkSegmentationLambda reviewing Security Group and Network Firewall rules for CDE isolation. PciRequirementMapper tagging each finding to a specific PCI-DSS 4.0 requirement ID (e.g., Req 7.2.1, Req 10.7.1). DeviationDetector comparing findings against compliance baseline. QSA Report Generator producing PDF with pre-populated SAQ D responses and CSV deviation summary.
reference.system_components: EventBridge Scheduler rule (quarterly cron, UTC) invoking Step Functions state machine. Step Functions Express Workflow with parallel branches for Config, IAM, LogRetention, and NetworkSegmentation scan workers, then a sequential ReportGenerator step. FastAPI control plane on Lambda via Mangum with API Gateway v2. PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ in production for scan metadata. S3 evidence bucket with SSE-KMS, versioning, and lifecycle to Glacier at 12 months. ComplianceBaselineLoader reading expected states from S3 JSON config files. ReportBuilder with Jinja2 QSA templates per PCI-DSS 4.0 requirement section. SNS topic for scan completion and critical deviation notifications.
reference.workflows: Quarterly Automated Scan: EventBridge Scheduler triggers Step Functions at 02:00 UTC on first day of each quarter. State machine fans out to 4 parallel scan Lambda workers, each writing findings to control_checks and evidence artifacts to S3. After all workers complete, ReportGeneratorLambda runs PciRequirementMapper against all findings, renders Jinja2 QSA template per requirement section, produces PDF via WeasyPrint and CSV deviation list, stores both to S3, updates qsa_questionnaires record, and fires SNS notification with report download URL to compliance team. Pre-Audit On-Demand Run: Compliance analyst calls POST /scans via Cognito-authenticated API, triggering same Step Functions workflow with on_demand trigger type. Progress visible via GET /scans/:id status polling.
reference.data_model: cde_components (id UUID, name, component_type ENUM ec2/rds/firewall/lambda, aws_resource_id VARCHAR(255), in_scope BOOLEAN, last_scanned_at TIMESTAMPTZ). scan_runs (id UUID, triggered_by ENUM scheduled/on_demand, status ENUM running/completed/partial_failure/failed, pci_version VARCHAR(10), started_at, completed_at). control_checks (id UUID, scan_run_id FK, cde_component_id FK, pci_requirement_id VARCHAR(10), check_type VARCHAR(100), result ENUM pass/fail/not_applicable, evidence_s3_key, checked_at). audit_findings (id UUID, control_check_id FK, severity ENUM critical/high/medium/low, description, expected_value, actual_value, status ENUM open/remediated, opened_at). qsa_questionnaires (id UUID, scan_run_id FK, pci_version, report_s3_key, csv_s3_key, generated_at). remediation_items (id UUID, audit_finding_id FK, owner, due_date DATE, status ENUM open/in_progress/closed, notes).
reference.api_design: FastAPI on Lambda via Mangum. POST /scans (trigger on-demand scan, returns scan_run_id). GET /scans (list history, paginated). GET /scans/:id (status and summary). GET /scans/:id/results (all control_checks with findings). GET /scans/:id/report?format=pdf|csv (presigned S3 download URL). GET /deviations?status=open (all open audit_findings across runs). GET /requirements/:pci_req_id/evidence (evidence artifacts for a specific PCI requirement). GET /cde-components (registry). PUT /cde-components/:id (update in-scope flag). Auth: API Gateway Lambda authorizer validating Cognito JWT. All endpoints require compliance_reader role minimum.
reference.security_and_compliance: Tool in PCI CDE scope — access controls and change management are themselves audit evidence. ScanWorkerRole IAM policy: Config:GetComplianceDetailsByConfigRule, Config:DescribeConfigRules, IAM:GetPolicy, IAM:ListAttachedRolePolicies, IAM:ListUsers, logs:FilterLogEvents, ec2:DescribeSecurityGroups, network-firewall:DescribeFirewallPolicy — no write permissions to any CDE resource. Evidence S3 bucket: SSE-KMS with dedicated KMS key, block all public access, object-level CloudTrail logging for GetObject and PutObject. Cognito user pool with password policy and MFA optional at admin discretion. CloudTrail trail with S3 data events covering evidence bucket. API Gateway access logs retained 1 year. Step Functions execution history 90 days.
reference.deployment_and_operations: GitLab CI pipeline: ruff lint, mypy type check, pytest with moto for AWS SDK mocking (80% coverage gate on scanner modules), Terraform plan against staging, manual approval gate for production Terraform apply. Lambda packages built as Python 3.12 zip with dependencies via pip install -t. Lambda function versions pinned in Terraform with previous version alias for rollback. EventBridge Scheduler enabled/disabled via Terraform variable for maintenance windows. Environments: dev (single-AZ RDS, mock scan data), staging (multi-AZ RDS, sandboxed AWS account), production (multi-AZ RDS, live CDE read access). Terraform state in S3 with DynamoDB lock.
reference.observability: CloudWatch custom dashboard: last_scan_run_status, open_deviations_count, critical_findings_count, days_since_last_successful_scan. Lambda metrics: invocation count, error rate, duration (p99). Step Functions: execution success rate, execution duration. SNS email alerts: scan failure (Step Functions execution FAILED), critical severity audit_finding created, report generation timeout (Lambda duration > 10 minutes). CloudTrail anomaly detection for unexpected CDE resource access by scan roles.
reference.cost_and_scaling: ~$150/month for quarterly scan cadence: Lambda invocations at quarterly scale are near-zero cost. RDS PostgreSQL db.t3.small Multi-AZ ~$80, S3 storage ~$20, Step Functions Express Workflows ~$10, API Gateway ~$10, CloudWatch ~$20. On-demand pre-audit runs add minor Lambda cost. WeasyPrint PDF generation is CPU-intensive — consider 3008MB Lambda memory for report generator to stay within 15-minute timeout for large CDE environments.
reference.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (8 weeks): CDE component registry, ConfigComplianceLambda for CDE segmentation rules, IamPolicyReviewLambda, evidence S3 storage, PciRequirementMapper for SAQ D requirements, CSV deviation report, Cognito auth, Terraform IaC, EventBridge quarterly schedule, GitLab CI pipeline. Phase 2 (4 weeks): LogRetentionCheckLambda, NetworkSegmentationLambda via Security Group rules, PDF QSA report generation with Jinja2/WeasyPrint, SNS notifications, DeviationDetector with baseline comparison. Phase 3 (4 weeks): Remediation tracking via remediation_items, Jira webhook integration for ticket creation, compliance posture dashboard via CloudWatch, AWS Network Firewall rule analysis.
reference.development_guidelines: Python ruff linting and mypy strict type checking enforced in GitLab CI. All scanner Lambda functions must have unit tests with moto mocking AWS SDK calls — no live AWS calls in tests. PciRequirementMapper logic unit-tested against all 12 PCI-DSS 4.0 requirement sections. Terraform modules for Lambda, Step Functions, and S3 in separate reusable modules. No hardcoded AWS resource IDs — all configuration via SSM Parameter Store or environment variables. Evidence artifact S3 keys follow pattern: {scan_run_id}/{cde_component_id}/{check_type}/{timestamp}.json.
reference.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: WeasyPrint PDF generation timing out for CDE environments with 100+ components. Mitigation: chunk report generation into per-requirement-section Lambda invocations and assemble via Step Functions. Risk: AWS Config rule coverage gaps for custom CDE components not tracked by managed rules. Mitigation: ComplianceBaselineLoader supports custom check definitions for non-Config-native resources. Tradeoff: FastAPI on Lambda over a persistent container — cold start latency is acceptable for infrequent on-demand scans. Tradeoff: RDS over DynamoDB for structured query support on scan history and requirement-level evidence lookup.
reference.open_questions_resolved: Network firewall rule export will use AWS Network Firewall DescribeFirewallPolicy API — no manual upload required. PDF format confirmed via WeasyPrint with Jinja2 templates; report generator Lambda allocated 3008MB to handle large CDE environments. Baseline configs stored in S3 JSON files editable by admin role, not hardcoded in Lambda.

  "thinking_summary": "The architecture plan aims to address the identified focus issues by enhancing the assignment lockout policy with embedding-similarity matching and defining a sliding window strategy for conversation context management to handle long tutoring sessions.
