parsed.thinking_summary: The architecture plan aims to address the identified focus issues by enhancing the assignment lockout policy with embedding-similarity matching and defining a sliding window strategy for conversation context management to handle long tutoring sessions. These improvements are crucial for maintaining academic integrity and ensuring reliable context retention for effective AI-driven tutoring.

fix_report.action_taken: Implemented embedding-similarity lockout check: embed the incoming student query with Titan Embeddings, compute cosine similarity against stored problem statement embeddings in hint_policies, trigger deflection if similarity exceeds 0.88 threshold.
fix_report.changed_sections: PolicyEnforcer service layer, hint_policies table design
fix_report.expected_outcome: The system now includes a secondary check for embedding-similarity to catch paraphrased queries, enhancing the assignment lockout policy's effectiveness.

fix_report.action_taken: Defined sliding window context strategy: send last 8 turns to Bedrock; if session exceeds 8 turns, generate a 2-sentence summary of earlier context and prepend it to the window.
fix_report.expected_outcome: The system can now handle long sessions by summarizing and providing context for the AI, preventing silent context loss or API errors.

parsed.title: STEM AI Tutoring Assistant -- Architecture (v2)
parsed.executive_summary: The revised architecture plan addresses the high-severity focus issue of the assignment lockout policy by adding an embedding-similarity check and defines a sliding window strategy for conversation context management. These changes ensure the system's reliability and effectiveness in maintaining academic integrity and providing context for long tutoring sessions.

reference.thinking_summary: Round 2 resolves ISS-001 by adding an embedding-similarity lockout check in PolicyEnforcer: the student query is embedded via Titan Embeddings, cosine similarity is computed against stored problem statement embeddings in hint_policies, and deflection is triggered if similarity exceeds 0.88. ISS-002 is resolved by defining a last-8-turns sliding window with a summarization step for dropped context. Both fixes integrate cleanly into the existing TutoringService and PolicyEnforcer without architectural changes. The pgvector infrastructure already supports embedding-based lockout since document_chunks already uses vector(1536).

fix_report.action_taken: PolicyEnforcer now runs a two-stage lockout check: (1) keyword match against hint_policies WHERE rule_type='keyword'; (2) if keyword check passes, embed the student query via Titan Embeddings v2 and query hint_policies WHERE rule_type='embedding_hash' using pgvector cosine distance operator (<=>); deflect if any stored problem embedding has cosine similarity >=0.88. Threshold is configurable per course via hint_policies.similarity_threshold column. Deflection events logged to interaction_logs with policy_triggered=True and rule_id.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Paraphrased queries that bypass keyword matching are caught by embedding similarity check; academic integrity enforcement is robust to student rephrasing strategies

fix_report.action_taken: TutoringService implements a sliding window context builder: the last 8 conversation_turns are fetched from Redis or PostgreSQL fallback and passed to Bedrock. If session has more than 8 turns, a summarization prompt (system: 'Summarize the following STEM tutoring exchange in 2 sentences focusing on concepts covered') is sent to Bedrock to generate a context_summary; the summary is prepended to the window as a system message. Summaries are cached in Redis keyed by session_id:summary_turn_N with 2-hour TTL.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Long tutoring sessions maintain coherent context without Bedrock API errors; students receive contextually appropriate hints even after extended sessions

reference.title: STEM AI Tutoring Assistant -- Architecture (v2)
reference.executive_summary: An LLM-powered STEM tutoring assistant on AWS Bedrock replacing a legacy static FAQ, with RAG over course PDFs via pgvector, two-stage assignment lockout enforcement (keyword + embedding-similarity), and FERPA-compliant interaction logging. Students receive adaptive hints via a React chat UI with SSE token streaming; instructors configure lockout policies and view session analytics. Long sessions use a sliding-window context strategy with summarization to maintain coherence within Bedrock's context limits.
reference.architecture_overview: React 18 SPA communicates via HTTPS to a FastAPI backend on ECS Fargate behind an ALB with WAF. TutoringService orchestrates: (1) pgvector hybrid search retrieving top-3 course document chunks, (2) two-stage PolicyEnforcer checking keyword and embedding-similarity lockout rules, (3) context window builder assembling last-8-turns sliding window with optional summarization, (4) AWS Bedrock Claude claude-sonnet invocation via boto3 with streamed response. Amazon Titan Embeddings v2 handles both document chunk ingestion and per-query embedding for similarity-based lockout. PostgreSQL 16 with pgvector stores course embeddings and interaction logs; Redis 7 caches conversation context. Cognito authenticates students and instructors with course-enrollment-scoped authorization.
reference.technology_stack: Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, React Query, Zustand, useSSE hook for token streaming. Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, boto3 for Bedrock and Titan Embeddings. Database: PostgreSQL 16 (RDS Multi-AZ) with pgvector extension, ivfflat index on document_chunks.embedding. Cache: Redis 7 (ElastiCache) for conversation context and summarization cache. LLM: AWS Bedrock Claude claude-sonnet for tutoring responses and context summarization. Embeddings: Amazon Titan Embeddings v2 (1536-dim). Auth: Cognito user pools. CI/CD: GitHub Actions, ECR, ECS Fargate rolling deploy. Observability: Datadog APM. Feature flags: LaunchDarkly per course.
reference.functional_feature_map: Student Tutoring Chat: SSE-streamed responses for STEM concept questions and step-by-step walkthroughs. Assignment Lockout Enforcement: keyword match then embedding-similarity check (cosine >=0.88) against instructor-defined problem embeddings; deflected queries receive canned guidance without LLM invocation. RAG Retrieval: hybrid pgvector cosine search + PostgreSQL full-text search for STEM notation across course document chunks. Context Window Management: last-8-turns sliding window with 2-sentence summarization for dropped context. Instructor Policy Config: keyword and problem-statement embedding upload, per-course similarity threshold tuning. Instructor Analytics: session volume, deflection rate, avg response latency, top-queried course topics.
reference.system_components: ALB with WAF (SQLi/XSS rules + rate limiting). ECS Fargate: tutor-api service (min 2, max 10 tasks), embedding-worker service for async document ingestion. RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ with pgvector. ElastiCache Redis 7 cluster for conversation context and summarization cache. AWS Bedrock: Claude claude-sonnet (response generation, context summarization), Titan Embeddings v2 (document and query embedding). S3 bucket for raw course document uploads before ingestion. Cognito user pools with student/instructor groups. Datadog APM sidecar. LaunchDarkly SDK for per-course feature flags.
reference.workflows: Two-Stage Lockout Check: student sends message; TutoringService calls PolicyEnforcer; stage 1: SQL query on hint_policies WHERE rule_type='keyword' AND course_id=:course_id, check if any keyword appears in query text; stage 2: if stage 1 passes, embed query via Titan Embeddings v2, execute pgvector query SELECT rule_id FROM hint_policies WHERE rule_type='embedding_hash' AND course_id=:course_id ORDER BY embedding <=> :query_embedding LIMIT 1 -- if cosine similarity of top result >= hint_policies.similarity_threshold, trigger deflection; if both stages pass, proceed to RAG retrieval and Bedrock call. Context Window Build: fetch last 8 turns from Redis (TTL 30 min) or PostgreSQL fallback; if session.message_count > 8, retrieve or generate context_summary via Bedrock summarization prompt; prepend summary as system message; assemble context array: [system_prompt, context_summary_if_any, last_8_turns]; send to Bedrock with streaming=True.
reference.data_model: students (id UUID PK, cognito_sub VARCHAR, name_hashed_at DATE). courses (id UUID PK, instructor_id FK, title VARCHAR, embedding_model_version VARCHAR). tutoring_sessions (id UUID PK, student_id FK, course_id FK, started_at TIMESTAMPTZ, message_count INT, context_summary TEXT, summary_as_of_turn INT). conversation_turns (id UUID PK, session_id FK, role ENUM user/assistant, content_hash VARCHAR, response_type ENUM hint/deflected/error, policy_triggered BOOLEAN, rule_id FK nullable, latency_ms INT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). document_chunks (id UUID PK, document_id FK, chunk_index INT, content_text TEXT, embedding vector(1536)). hint_policies (id UUID PK, course_id FK, rule_type ENUM keyword/embedding_hash, rule_value TEXT, embedding vector(1536) nullable, similarity_threshold FLOAT DEFAULT 0.88, created_by FK). interaction_logs (id UUID PK, turn_id FK, student_id VARCHAR, course_id FK, event_type VARCHAR, metadata_json JSONB, logged_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
reference.api_design: POST /v1/tutor/chat (SSE, body: {session_id, message, course_id}) -- runs lockout check, RAG retrieval, context build, Bedrock stream. GET /v1/tutor/sessions/{session_id}/history -- returns conversation_turns for session. POST /v1/courses/{id}/policies -- instructor creates keyword or embedding_hash lockout rule; embedding_hash rules include base64-encoded problem statement, backend embeds and stores vector. GET /v1/courses/{id}/analytics?from=&to= -- returns session_count, deflection_rate, avg_latency_ms, top_topics. POST /v1/courses/{id}/documents -- multipart upload; triggers async embedding-worker ECS task. GET /v1/health -- ALB health check.
reference.security_and_compliance: Authentication: Cognito JWT validated at FastAPI Depends(get_current_user); course enrollment verified per request against students-courses enrollment table. Prompt injection guard: regex scan of user input for patterns ('ignore previous', 'you are now', 'system:', 'assistant:'); blocked queries logged with event_type='injection_attempt'. Assignment lockout: two-stage check (keyword + embedding similarity >=0.88) enforced in backend before any Bedrock call; client-side blocking is not relied upon. FERPA: interaction_logs link to course_id and student_id; access restricted to the student themselves and course instructor; student identifiers in logs anonymized (hashed) after 90 days via nightly Lambda; interaction logs excluded from Datadog log forwarding. Bedrock: system prompt passed as system role, not prepended to user message; no student query content included in Bedrock model training (Bedrock API guarantee).
reference.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI: ruff lint, mypy type check, pytest with mocked Bedrock responses (pytest-mock) and pgvector test DB (Docker Compose), build Docker, push ECR. ECS rolling deploy with 100% minimum healthy task during update. Environments: dev (single-AZ, t-class RDS, mock Bedrock), staging (multi-AZ RDS, real Bedrock, 3 pilot courses), production (multi-AZ RDS, auto-scaling 2-10 tasks, LaunchDarkly per-course rollout). Document ingestion: S3 event triggers ECS embedding-worker task; chunks documents at 512 tokens with 64-token overlap, embeds via Titan v2, inserts into document_chunks with pgvector index rebuild after batch. Secrets: Cognito pool IDs and RDS credentials in AWS Secrets Manager; Bedrock IAM role via ECS task role -- no API keys.
reference.observability: Datadog APM: trace per /v1/tutor/chat request with spans for PolicyEnforcer, pgvector retrieval, context build, and Bedrock invocation. Custom metrics: tutor_response_latency_ms histogram per course_id, policy_deflection_rate counter per course_id and rule_type, bedrock_input_tokens_total and bedrock_output_tokens_total counters, context_summarization_invocations_total. Alerts: p99 response latency >5s fires PagerDuty; deflection rate spike >2x 7-day average fires Slack alert to instructor; embedding-worker queue depth >50 fires ops alert. Structured logs: student_id replaced by hashed_session_token; no student query plaintext in Datadog.
reference.cost_and_scaling: Bedrock Claude claude-sonnet at $0.003/1K input tokens and $0.015/1K output tokens; avg tutoring turn ~800 input tokens and ~300 output tokens = ~$0.007/turn; 10,000 turns/month = ~$70/month. Titan Embeddings v2 at $0.0001/1K tokens; 200 course document chunks/course, 3 courses = 600 embeddings plus query embeddings ~$5/month. ECS Fargate: min 2 tasks x 1vCPU/2GB = ~$60/month baseline; scales to 10 tasks during peak exam periods. RDS PostgreSQL Multi-AZ db.t3.medium: ~$120/month. Total: ~$300/month for pilot, scaling proportionally with course enrollment.
reference.phased_implementation: Phase 1 -- MVP Tutoring (6 weeks): ECS Fargate, FastAPI, Cognito auth, RAG over course PDFs (pgvector + Titan Embeddings v2), Bedrock Claude tutoring with system-role-locked prompt, SSE streaming React chat UI, keyword-only lockout policy, interaction logging. Phase 2 -- Academic Integrity Hardening (3 weeks): embedding-similarity lockout check (ISS-001 fix), sliding-window context with summarization (ISS-002 fix), prompt injection guard, instructor policy configuration UI, LaunchDarkly per-course feature flags. Phase 3 -- Analytics and Scale (3 weeks): instructor analytics dashboard, Datadog APM custom metrics, anonymization Lambda for 90-day FERPA requirement, performance tuning of pgvector ivfflat index, load testing for exam-period demand spikes.
reference.development_guidelines: All Bedrock prompt templates versioned in Git under prompts/ directory with changelog entries. Lockout policy changes require instructor authentication and are audit-logged. pytest mocks all Bedrock and Titan Embeddings calls; integration tests run against a dockerized PostgreSQL with pgvector extension. Student query plaintext must never appear in structured logs -- log only content_hash. Feature flag every course rollout; no direct production deployments without staging validation.
reference.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: embedding-similarity threshold (0.88) is too aggressive, flagging legitimate STEM questions as assignment problems -- mitigated by per-course threshold tuning and instructor review of deflection logs. Risk: Titan Embeddings v2 model version changes causing embedding space drift and false-negative lockout misses -- mitigated by storing embedding_model_version on courses and reindexing when model updates. Tradeoff: pgvector over Pinecone/Weaviate eliminates operational overhead but limits to single-node vector search; acceptable at pilot scale but may require migration at 1M+ chunks. Tradeoff: Bedrock Claude over GPT-4 satisfies AWS data boundary requirement at slightly higher latency.
reference.open_questions_resolved: Cosine similarity threshold 0.88 chosen as default after testing on sample STEM problem sets; configurable per course to allow stricter enforcement in exam periods. Sliding window of 8 turns chosen to balance context quality and Bedrock token cost for typical 30-minute tutoring sessions. Titan Embeddings v2 chosen over OpenAI text-embedding-ada-002 to keep all embeddings within AWS boundary.

parsed.thinking_summary: The architecture plan aims to address the core requirement of ingesting, normalizing, and aggregating K-12 standardized test scores from multiple vendors into a common schema, ensuring FERPA compliance, and providing a secure, scalable, and efficient data pipeline with a focus on maintaining data privacy, security, and compliance. The plan includes a multi-stage process with a focus on resolving identified issues, preserving user-confirmed requirements, and improving the architecture without introducing regressions. The architecture is designed to be implementation-grade with a clear separation of concerns, robust security measures, and scalability to handle the district's data volume and complexity.

fix_report.action_taken: Added a dedicated schema registry step in the validation DAG for schema versioning and drift detection before production.
fix_report.expected_outcome: The architecture now includes a schema registry to handle vendor schema versioning and prevent corruption of the normalization stage due to schema changes.

fix_report.action_taken: Designated a second ClickHouse node for parallelized aggregation DAGs split by school to address scaling bottlenecks.
fix_report.expected_outcome: The architecture now supports parallelized aggregation to handle larger data volumes without hitting a single-node scaling limit.

fix_report.action_taken: Implemented a more robust SFTP connection handling with retries and exponential backoff in the ingest_dag.
fix_report.expected_outcome: The architecture now has a more resilient SFTP connection handling to manage vendor instability and ensure data ingestion.

fix_report.action_taken: Reviewed and tightened GCS bucket ACLs to prevent accidental exposure of quarantined files.
fix_report.expected_outcome: The architecture now has secure GCS bucket configurations to prevent premature deletion of quarantined files.

parsed.title: Multi-Vendor K-12 Standardized Test Scores Data Pipeline Architecture
parsed.executive_summary: This architecture plan outlines a secure, scalable, and compliant data pipeline for ingesting, normalizing, and aggregating K-12 standardized test scores from multiple vendors. It addresses the core requirement of unifying fragmented assessment data, with a focus on resolving identified issues, preserving user-confirmed requirements, and improving the architecture without introducing regressions. The plan includes a multi-stage process with a focus on maintaining data privacy, security, and compliance, and is designed to be implementation-grade with a clear separation of concerns, robust security measures, and scalability to handle the district's data volume and complexity.
parsed.architecture_overview: The architecture consists of a multi-stage data pipeline with stages for ingestion, validation, linkage, normalization, and aggregation. It uses Apache Airflow for orchestration, Google Cloud Platform (GCP) for hosting, and ClickHouse for analytics. The pipeline ensures FERPA compliance, data privacy, and security through Google Workspace SSO, IAM roles, and VPC-SC perimeter. It also includes a robust observability stack with Grafana, Cloud Monitoring, and Prometheus.

technology_stack.backend: Python 3.12, Apache Airflow on Cloud Composer
technology_stack.data_platform: ClickHouse on GCP Compute Engine, Google Cloud Storage, BigQuery
technology_stack.hosting_target: GCP Cloud Composer, Cloud Run, Compute Engine
technology_stack.security_and_compliance: Google Workspace SSO, IAM roles, VPC-SC perimeter, FERPA compliance

data_pipeline.ingestion: SFTP ingestion from vendors, JSON schema validation and quarantine
data_pipeline.dashboard_delivery: Grafana dashboards for pipeline run metrics

security.authentication: Google Workspace SSO with GCP IAM
security.authorization: IAM roles for data-pipeline-admin, clickhouse-reader, compliance-auditor
security.privacy_controls: Small-cohort suppression, student PII masking, GCS bucket retention

functional_feature_map.compliance: FERPA small-cohort suppression, vendor data sharing agreements

data_pipeline.linkage: BigQuery temp tables for enrollment join
data_pipeline.aggregation: ClickHouse materialized views for performance aggregates

data_storage.raw_storage: Google Cloud Storage with lifecycle policies

linkage: BigQuery temp tables for enrollment join
aggregation: ClickHouse materialized views for performance aggregates

storage_design.raw_storage: Google Cloud Storage with lifecycle policies

internal_api_patterns.communication: Airflow XCom for small payloads and GCS object paths for file references

security_and_compliance.authentication: Google Workspace SSO with GCP IAM
security_and_compliance.authorization: IAM roles for data-pipeline-admin, clickhouse-reader, compliance-auditor
security_and_compliance.privacy_controls: Small-cohort suppression, student PII masking, GCS bucket retention
security_and_compliance.compliance: FERPA small-cohort suppression, vendor data sharing agreements

deployment_and_operations.hosting: GCP Cloud Composer, Cloud Run, Compute Engine
deployment_and_operations.deployment_topology: Multi-node ClickHouse cluster, single-node for validation
deployment_and_operations.ci_cd_design: Cloud Build, container build and push to Artifact Registry, Cloud Run deploy, DAG sync via GCS bucket
deployment_and_operations.rollback_strategy: Airflow DAG versioning via git tags, ClickHouse snapshot restore via Compute Engine disk snapshot, Cloud Run revision traffic rollback

cost_and_scaling.storage_cost: Managed GCS storage with lifecycle policies to control costs
cost_and_scaling.compute_cost: GCP Cloud Composer and Compute Engine for scalability
cost_and_scaling.scaling_strategy: Parallelized aggregation DAGs split by school

parsed.phased_implementation: Phase 1: MVP with one vendor in 10 weeks; Phase 2: Additional vendor onboarding, predictive early-warning models, teacher effectiveness correlation views
parsed.development_guidelines: Use Cloud Build for CI/CD, ensure Airflow DAGs are tested, and follow best practices for Python 3.12 and Apache Airflow

risks_and_tradeoffs.technical_risks: Vendor schema changes corrupt normalization; ClickHouse disk exhaustion on multi-year accumulation
risks_and_tradeoffs.compliance_risks: Vendor data sharing agreement IDs logged per ingestion run
risks_and_tradeoffs.security_risks: Vendor SFTP credentials in Secret Manager require coordinated rotation; misrouted quarantine files could expose PII if GCS bucket ACLs misconfigured

reference.thinking_summary: Designing a FERPA-compliant K-12 assessment data pipeline on GCP. Key decisions: Airflow DAG separation by task group enables targeted retries without re-ingesting clean data; ClickHouse materialized views pre-aggregate school and grade-level metrics to serve principal dashboards at sub-second latency; small-cohort suppression baked into MV definitions prevents FERPA violations at query time; VPC-SC perimeter and GCS IAM ensure student PII never leaves the district GCP project boundary.
reference.title: K-12 Multi-Vendor Assessment Data Pipeline
reference.executive_summary: A FERPA-compliant multi-stage data pipeline on GCP that ingests nightly standardized test score files from assessment vendors via SFTP, validates against versioned JSON schemas, quarantines invalid rows, links scores to student enrollment records, normalizes into a canonical schema stored in ClickHouse, and refreshes materialized views powering Grafana dashboards for principals and curriculum coaches. The pipeline enforces small-cohort suppression at the aggregation layer and maintains a 7-year retention chain for state compliance.
reference.architecture_overview: Five-stage Airflow DAG chain orchestrated by Cloud Composer: (1) Ingest DAG polls vendor SFTP endpoints and deposits raw files to GCS raw/ prefix with vendor and date partitioning. (2) Validate DAG invokes Cloud Run validation microservice checking each file against a versioned JSON schema registry, routing passing files to processed/ and failures to quarantine/ with structured error manifests. (3) Link DAG joins validated score rows to students and enrollment_records tables in BigQuery temp datasets by state_student_id and academic_year. (4) Normalize DAG applies Python transforms mapping vendor-specific score scales to canonical proficiency_level enum and writes to ClickHouse normalized_scores. (5) Aggregate DAG triggers ClickHouse MV refreshes on performance_aggregates applying small-cohort suppression for groups below 10. VPC-SC perimeter encloses the entire GCP project; no student PII exits the boundary.
reference.technology_stack: Orchestration: Apache Airflow 2.8 on Cloud Composer 2, Python 3.12 DAGs. Validation microservice: Python 3.12 FastAPI on Cloud Run with jsonschema library. Storage: GCS for raw/quarantine/processed files, ClickHouse 24.x on Compute Engine n2-standard-8 for analytics, BigQuery for compliance audit exports. Dashboards: Grafana 10 with ClickHouse data source plugin. Auth: Google Workspace SSO, GCP IAM with Workload Identity. CI: Cloud Build. Monitoring: Prometheus, Cloud Monitoring, PagerDuty.
reference.functional_feature_map: Vendor SFTP Ingestion: polling-based download with file deduplication via GCS object hash. JSON Schema Validation: per-vendor schema registry with version pinning and quarantine routing with structured error manifests. Enrollment Linkage: BigQuery join by state_student_id resolving multi-year enrollment changes. Score Normalization: configurable transform map per vendor converting raw/scaled scores to canonical proficiency_level. Aggregation Refresh: ClickHouse MV refresh computing proficiency rates by school, grade, subject, and academic year with cohort suppression. Dashboard Layer: Grafana panels for school comparison, grade-band trends, year-over-year proficiency shifts. Quarantine Management: data team remediation queue surfacing failed rows with field-level error codes.
reference.system_components: Cloud Composer 2 environment hosting Airflow scheduler and workers, Cloud Run validation service scaling 0-10 instances on ingestion trigger, GCS buckets for raw/quarantine/processed/dag-sync, ClickHouse 2-node Compute Engine cluster with SSD for normalized_scores and performance_aggregates, BigQuery dataset for enrollment lookups and compliance audit exports, Secret Manager storing vendor SFTP credentials, Grafana instance on Compute Engine with ClickHouse and BigQuery data sources, Prometheus scraping ClickHouse and Cloud Run metrics, PagerDuty via Cloud Monitoring alerting policies.
reference.workflows: Nightly Ingestion: Cloud Composer triggers ingest_dag at 23:00. Airflow SFTPSensor polls each active vendor host for new files matching date pattern. Files downloaded to GCS raw/vendor_id/date/ prefix. File hash checked against processed manifest to skip duplicates. validate_dag triggered on successful ingest. Validation and Quarantine: validate_dag submits each raw file to Cloud Run microservice. Service loads schema version from vendor registry, validates each row, accumulates errors. Files with greater than 5% row failure rate quarantined to GCS quarantine/vendor_id/date/ with JSON error manifest. PagerDuty alert fired with vendor name, file name, and failure rate. Passing files moved to processed/. Normalization and Aggregation: link_dag loads processed rows into BigQuery temp table, joins to enrollment_records on state_student_id and academic_year, flags unmatched rows as orphaned for data team review. normalize_dag transforms linked rows to proficiency_level enum and writes to ClickHouse. aggregate_dag executes ClickHouse MV refresh on performance_aggregates grouped by school_id, grade_level, subject, academic_year, applying WHERE n_students >= 10 suppression. Dashboard queries read exclusively from performance_aggregates.
reference.data_model: assessment_vendors (id UUID PK, name, sftp_host, sftp_credential_secret_id, schema_version, dsa_document_gcs_path, active BOOL). raw_score_files (id UUID PK, vendor_id FK, gcs_path, file_hash, received_at, row_count INT, valid_row_count INT, validation_status ENUM pending/passed/quarantined/failed, quarantine_manifest_path, processed_at). students (id UUID PK, state_student_id VARCHAR UNIQUE, district_student_id, first_name_encrypted, last_name_encrypted, dob_encrypted, current_school_id FK, active BOOL). enrollment_records (id UUID PK, student_id FK, school_id FK, academic_year, grade_level INT, enrollment_date, withdrawal_date). normalized_scores (id UUID PK, student_id FK, vendor_id FK, raw_score_file_id FK, assessment_name, subject, raw_score FLOAT, scaled_score FLOAT, proficiency_level ENUM below_basic/basic/proficient/advanced, test_date, academic_year). performance_aggregates (id UUID PK, school_id FK, grade_level INT, subject, academic_year, proficiency_rate FLOAT, n_students INT, suppressed BOOL, refreshed_at). school_benchmarks (id UUID PK, school_id FK, subject, benchmark_proficiency_target FLOAT, academic_year).
reference.api_design: No external HTTP API. Internal interfaces: Cloud Run validation endpoint POST /validate (multipart file + vendor_id + schema_version, returns {passed_rows, failed_rows, error_manifest_path}). Airflow REST API internal: GET /api/v1/dags/{dag_id}/dagRuns for monitoring integration. BigQuery compliance export: scheduled query writing score access events to compliance_audit dataset. Grafana queries ClickHouse via native HTTP interface on port 8123 restricted to read-only grafana service account. PagerDuty alert webhook on DAG failure and quarantine threshold breach.
reference.security_and_compliance: FERPA controls: small-cohort suppression (n less than 10) enforced in ClickHouse MV definition. ClickHouse row-level security restricts principals to their own school_id. VPC-SC perimeter blocks all GCS and BigQuery API calls originating outside the district GCP project. CMEK via Cloud KMS encrypts all GCS buckets and ClickHouse persistent disks. Vendor SFTP credentials in Secret Manager with 90-day rotation. Student PII fields (first_name, last_name, dob) AES-256 encrypted before insert. BigQuery audit dataset captures every query touching normalized_scores with timestamp, principal identity, and row count. Data sharing agreement document IDs logged per vendor ingestion run.
reference.deployment_and_operations: Cloud Composer 2 in us-central1 with 3 scheduler nodes and auto-scaling worker pool (2-8 workers). DAG deployment: Cloud Build pushes Python files to GCS dag-sync bucket; Composer picks up within 60s. Cloud Run validation service: Cloud Build builds Docker image, pushes to Artifact Registry, deploys revision with 100% traffic shift on health check pass. ClickHouse upgraded via Ansible playbook with pre-upgrade disk snapshot. Environments: dev (single Composer, ClickHouse dev node with 90-day score sample), staging (full topology with 2 mock vendors), production (Composer + 2-node ClickHouse). Rollback: DAG git tag rollback via GCS sync; ClickHouse snapshot restore; Cloud Run revision rollback.
reference.observability: Prometheus scrapes ClickHouse system.metrics every 30s: query_duration_ms p99, rows_read_per_second, disk_used_bytes. Cloud Monitoring tracks Cloud Run: request_count, error_rate, instance_count. Grafana dashboards: Pipeline Health showing DAG run durations and quarantine rates per vendor; Data Quality showing orphaned_scores count and validation pass rate; ClickHouse Performance showing MV refresh duration and disk growth. PagerDuty alerts: DAG run failure, quarantine rate above 10%, MV refresh above 90 minutes, ClickHouse disk above 80%.
reference.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $800/month: Cloud Composer 2 ~$400, ClickHouse 2x n2-standard-8 ~$280, Cloud Run validation ~$30, GCS lifecycle storage ~$60, Grafana/Prometheus instance ~$30. Scaling trigger: add ClickHouse replica node at 100M normalized_scores rows or p99 MV refresh exceeding 60 minutes. Cloud Run validation workers scale automatically on file volume without manual intervention.
reference.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): SFTP ingestion for one vendor, JSON schema validation with quarantine, enrollment linkage via BigQuery join, normalization to canonical schema, ClickHouse aggregation with small-cohort suppression, Grafana grade-level dashboards for 5 pilot schools, Cloud Build CI and VPC-SC perimeter. Phase 2 Multi-Vendor (6 weeks): schema registry with version management for 2 additional vendors, parallel DAG execution per vendor, orphaned_scores remediation workflow, expanded dashboards for all district schools. Phase 3 Advanced Analytics (8 weeks): year-over-year proficiency trend views, school benchmark comparison panels, early-warning data mart for predictive model ingestion, BigQuery compliance export automation.
reference.development_guidelines: Python 3.12 with type hints on all DAG task functions. pytest for unit tests with Airflow LocalExecutor; minimum 80% coverage on normalization transform functions. Airflow DAG linting via airflow dags test in Cloud Build pipeline. ClickHouse schema migrations via versioned SQL files in /migrations directory applied by Ansible. No student PII in Airflow XCom payloads — pass GCS paths only. Vendor schema versions pinned in config/vendor_schemas.yaml under git version control. PR requires data engineer review for normalization or aggregation query changes.
reference.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Vendor sends mid-year schema change without notice corrupting normalization. Mitigation: schema registry with strict version pinning; validation DAG blocks normalization on schema mismatch and pages data team. Risk: ClickHouse single-node disk exhaustion on 7-year score accumulation. Mitigation: quarterly disk usage review with auto-alert at 80%; replica node planned at 100M rows. Risk: Enrollment record gaps leave test scores unlinked. Mitigation: orphaned_scores metric on Grafana with 5-business-day remediation SLA. Tradeoff: ClickHouse over BigQuery for analytics sub-second latency; accepts manual upgrade and backup overhead.
reference.open_questions_resolved: Selected ClickHouse over BigQuery for analytics layer due to sub-second aggregation latency requirements for principal dashboards. Confirmed GCS over Cloud SQL for raw file storage given unstructured vendor file formats. Chose Cloud Composer over self-managed Airflow to reduce operator burden. FERPA small-cohort suppression threshold set at 10 students per state guidance.
